docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:51:- **Alternative rejected:** Squash or filter execute-bead history to keep `git log` clean, and auto-discard timed-out iteration refs. Rejected because it destroys evidence and forecloses on manual conflict resolution when an agent's work is salvageable.
docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:52:- **Tradeoff:** Git history carries more noise (per-attempt commits, preserved refs) and users must occasionally garbage-collect by hand. In exchange, no agent action is unrecoverable and conflict resolution always has the original commits to refer back to.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-ebf3c09b/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"erik","body":"Bead ddx-ebf3c09b — repo-wide verb migration + agent_*.go cleanup
===================================================================

NO CHANGES committed in this attempt.

## Why this bead is currently blocked

The bead description has four parts. Three of the four are blocked on a
prerequisite that has not landed:

(a) "Delete DDx reimplementations of upstream subcommands"
    (agent_list/capabilities/doctor/check/models/providers/route_status/
    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay)

(c) "Register DDx overrides on mounted subtree (deprecations + condense)"

Both presuppose a mounted upstream Cobra root grafted under `ddx agent`
— i.e. `cli(ddx).agent(load_cli(agent))` per FEAT-001-cli.md:69 and
plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md:28.

The CONTRACT-003 audit (committed 08a40b69) explicitly identifies this
as a gap (docs/helix/02-design/contract-003-audit-2026-04-29.md:23–89):

    > DDx cannot write `agentcli.NewRootCommand()` against the current
    > contract.
    >
    > The plan's "delete with prejudice" list (agent_list,
    > agent_capabilities, agent_doctor, agent_check, agent_models,
    > agent_providers, agent_route_status, agent_catalog,
    > agent_reindex) presupposes that upstream owns these subcommands
    > inside a mountable tree. Today they exist only as flag-driven
    > branches in cmd/agent/main.go that DDx cannot consume
    > programmatically.
    >
    > Plan bead #6 ("open CONTRACT-003 amendment in `~/Projects/agent`
    > if #5 surfaces gaps") is **required, not conditional**, because
    > of this gap.

Concretely:

  * cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (lines 123–141) wires every soon-to-be-deleted
    file via `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentCheckCommand())`,
    `newAgentModelsCommand()`, `newAgentProvidersCommand()`,
    `newAgentRouteStatusCommand()`, `newAgentListCommand()`,
    `newAgentCapabilitiesCommand()`, `newAgentDoctorCommand()`,
    `newAgentBenchmarkCommand()`, `newAgentCatalogCommand()`,
    `newAgentReplayCommand()`. Deleting any of those `agent_*.go`
    files without the upstream `MountCLI` replacement would leave
    agent_cmd.go referencing missing constructors and break the build.
  * The replacement (`cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`) requires
    the upstream module to expose `MountCLI` from a non-`internal/`
    package, which the audit specifies as the upstream amendment
    CONTRACT-003-A (port `cmd/agent/main.go` from `flag` to `cobra`,
    move into `agent/cli`, re-export `MountCLI`, bump module version).
    That work has not happened in `~/Projects/agent`.

(b) "Migrate DDx-only subcommands"
    (agent_executions → tries_*; agent_log → runs_log;
     agent_metrics → runs_metrics; agent_usage folded into runs_metrics;
     agent_workers → work_workers)

This portion is mechanically possible inside DDx alone, but per the
plan (line 208) beads #3, #7, #8, #15 must merge atomically — with #15
landing in isolation, the new namespaces (`ddx runs`, `ddx tries`,
`ddx work workers`) need top-level registration that has no analogue
yet in command_factory_commands.go. The implementation P2 phase of the
plan (lines 196–204) lists "CLI: `ddx runs` / `ddx tries` / `ddx work
workers` namespaces (subsuming migrated subcommands)" as separate work
that comes after the frame/design beads. Doing only (b) here, without
(a) and (c), produces a confusing CLI surface where the same evidence
is reachable both as `ddx agent executions` and `ddx tries list`, and
it strands the deprecation messages that need to point at the new
verbs.

(d) "Update scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to
    new verbs"

This depends on the new verbs (`ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx runs *`,
`ddx tries *`, `ddx work workers *`) actually existing as runnable
commands so that the updated scripts/skills run green. `ddx run` and
`ddx try` are not registered today (grep for `Use:.*\"run\"` /
`Use:.*\"try\"` against cli/cmd/*.go shows only the `ddx agent run`
inner command). Updating scripts/eval-skill.sh,
scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh, scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh,
scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh,
library/.agents/skills/ddx-refine-plan/SKILL.md, etc. to call
non-existent verbs would break those flows immediately and would
specifically break the in-flight `ddx-refine-plan` skill that produced
this very plan (plan line 208).

## What an unblocked attempt needs

Roughly in dependency order:

1. Upstream amendment CONTRACT-003-A merged in `~/Projects/agent`:
   port to cobra, move commands to `agent/cli`, export `MountCLI`,
   tag a release, and bump `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` in
   DDx's go.mod (no replace directive — see memory
   feedback_no_local_replace).
2. Top-level `ddx run` and `ddx try` registered as first-class
   commands under CommandFactory (the implementation P2 thread of the
   plan).
3. `ddx runs`, `ddx tries`, `ddx work workers` namespace skeletons
   registered, even if their subcommand implementations are then
   ported in this bead.

Once those are in, the present bead becomes:

  * Replace the 11+ enumerated `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgent*Command())`
    calls in agent_cmd.go with `cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`,
    plus deprecation override registrations for `agent run`,
    `agent execute-bead`, `agent execute-loop`, and the override for
    `agent condense` (DDx-specific filter).
  * `git rm` agent_check.go, agent_models.go, agent_providers.go,
    agent_route_status.go (the only "delete with prejudice" files
    that actually exist in DDx today; agent_list/capabilities/doctor/
    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay only exist as inline functions
    inside agent_cmd.go and would be removed by the agent_cmd.go
    rewrite).
  * Move agent_executions.go → tries_*.go; agent_metrics.go →
    runs_metrics.go (folding agent_usage.go into it);
    agent_workers.go → work_workers.go; rename `newAgentLogCommand`
    in agent_cmd.go into `runs_log.go`. Update tests in lockstep.
  * Sweep scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to
    swap old verbs for new (e.g. `ddx agent run` → `ddx run`,
    `ddx agent execute-loop` → `ddx work`,
    `ddx agent execute-bead` → `ddx try`,
    `ddx agent log/metrics/usage/executions/workers` → the new
    `runs/tries/work workers` paths). Specific known references:
    scripts/eval-skill.sh, scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh,
    scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh, scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh,
    library/.agents/skills/ddx-refine-plan/SKILL.md,
    library/personas/README.md, cli/cmd/init.go:757,
    cli/cmd/bead_review.go:30–33, plus a wider grep across
    docs/**/*.md and library/**/*.md.

## What is done in this attempt

Nothing on disk — but the verification above (audit cross-reference,
agent_cmd.go wiring inspection, FEAT-001 surface inspection,
search for top-level `run`/`try` registrations) is captured here so
the next attempt can move directly into execution once bead #6 lands.

## Recommended next steps

- Park this bead behind bead #6 (upstream CONTRACT-003-A amendment)
  and the P2 implementation beads that introduce top-level
  `ddx run` / `ddx try` and the `runs`/`tries`/`work workers`
  namespace skeletons.
- Re-attempt with the same id once those prerequisites have merged.
  At that point this bead is mechanical: a contained refactor of
  agent_cmd.go plus a directed grep-and-edit sweep of the call sites
  enumerated above.

FIXED (5 of 8):
- health: HealthStatus! added (line 791)
- ready: ReadyStatus! added (line 794)
- workerLog added (returns WorkerLog! not String! — acceptable, richer)
- workerPrompt added
- agentCoordinators added (renamed to coordinators — semantically equivalent)

NOT FIXED (3 of 8):
1. documentByPath(path: String!): Document — STILL MISSING. GET /api/documents/{path...} remains orphaned.
2. Per-field doc comments — STILL MOSTLY MISSING. New types added in this commit (CoordinatorMetrics, HealthStatus, ReadyCheck, ReadyStatus, WorkerLog, LandOutcomeSummary, etc.) got per-field # comments, but the original 30+ types (NodeInfo, Project, Bead, Dependency, Document, DocumentReview, DocumentExecDef, ExecutorSpec, ResultSpec, MetricResultSpec, Evaluation, Thresholds, DocGraph, StaleReason, Commit, Worker, CurrentAttemptInfo, PhaseTransition, LastAttemptInfo, WorkerExecutionResult, AgentSession, TokenUsage, Persona, ExecutionRun, ExecutionDefinition, Provenance, MetricObservation, Provider, PageInfo, SearchResult, all Edge/Connection types) are untouched. 10-field sample from those types: 0/10 have per-field comments. Additionally, gqlgen does not emit # comments as GraphQL descriptions — the spec requires """...""" blocks. Recommend converting all to triple-quote descriptions.
3. search: [SearchResult!]! — STILL a plain list. SearchResultConnection/SearchResultEdge do not exist in the file.

REGRESSION:
- coordinatorMetricsByProject(projectID: ID!): CoordinatorMetrics! was silently dropped. GET /api/coordinator/metrics/{projectRoot} loses direct coverage.

VERDICT: REJECT. Retry with stronger model.

NEXT RETRY SHOULD:
1. Add documentByPath query
2. Add per-field descriptions (""" blocks, not # comments) to EVERY field in EVERY type. Start from ORIGINAL types not the new ones. Biggest lift — the model needs to grind through ~35 types.
3. Replace "search: [SearchResult!]!" with SearchResultConnection and add SearchResultConnection/SearchResultEdge types next to other connection types.
4. Restore coordinatorMetricsByProject(projectID) query.
5. attempt whose diff contained only an execution result.json. A later
attempt landed the actual code in commit 7c1ff6a5
("feat(graphql): enforce per-project allowlist on ts-net operator-prompt
submit [ddx-f3c7149d]") and merge commit 804f6dcf, both of which are
already in HEAD's history (base 75e0d9472387 includes them).

AC mapping (all in cli/internal/server/graphql/):

1. Approve mutation transitions proposed -> open (trusted only):
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:398 OperatorPromptApprove
     - requires CSRF, requires canAutoApprove(ident) for the caller
     - rejects non-operator-prompt or non-proposed beads
     - sets bead.StatusOpen and appends OperatorPromptApproveEventKind
   Test: resolver_operator_prompt_lifecycle_test.go
     TestOperatorPromptApprove_TransitionsProposedToOpen

2. Cancel transitions proposed -> cancelled:
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:446 OperatorPromptCancel
     - sets bead.StatusCancelled and appends OperatorPromptCancelEventKind
   Test: TestOperatorPromptCancel_TransitionsProposedToCancelled

3. auto-approve config skips proposed for configured localhost identity
   only and never for ts-net by default:
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:533 canAutoApprove
     - returns false unconditionally when ident.kind != "localhost"
     - empty allowlist -> false; "localhost" sentinel matches any actor
     OperatorPromptSubmit branch at resolver_operator_prompt.go:361 only
     calls canAutoApprove when input.AutoApprove=true.
   Tests:
     TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveDeniedForTsnet
     TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveAllowedForLocalhost
     TestOperatorPromptApprove_DeniedWhenIdentityNotOnAllowlist

4. Per-project allowlist enforced; non-listed ts-net peers get 403 on
   submit:
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:513 canSubmit
     - localhost always allowed; ts-net only when actor in allowlist
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:233 errSubmitDenied (status 403)
   Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_TsnetIdentityNotInAllowlistDenied

5. Config wiring:
     server/server.go:116-170 operatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist field +
     parseOperatorPromptAllowlistEnv(DDX_OPERATOR_PROMPT_ALLOWLIST)
     server/server.go:4709 plumbed into Resolver
     SetOperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist setter for tests

Why this bead came back with REVIEW:BLOCK
-----------------------------------------
The file change actually landed in commit 4e078e38
("fix(ui): align CommandPalette tokens with sidebar nav pattern [ddx-e1723c99]"),
which is an ancestor of base-rev aeaa8f1b. The reviewer flagged the bead
because the *most recent* execute attempt's reviewable diff contained only
a result.json execution artifact — the .svelte edits were not in that
particular attempt's diff because they had already been merged in a prior
attempt. The state of the source file on disk, however, is correct.

What a follow-up would need
---------------------------
Nothing in the source. If the orchestrator's review step is comparing the
attempt's diff (rather than the file state) against the AC, this bead will
keep re-queuing indefinitely. Two options:

  a) Mark the bead resolved by reference to commit 4e078e38, since the
     three AC items are observably present in the source.
  b) Adjust the review gate so it inspects file state at HEAD, not just
     the diff produced by the latest execute-bead attempt.

Producing a synthetic edit-and-revert just to populate a non-empty diff
would be dishonest and would muddy the file's history; not doing that.

Linked from skills/ddx/SKILL.md intent-router.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:05:31.000159751Z","description":"Create skills/ddx/reference/beads.md — the domain reference file a harness reads when the user asks to create, plan, or update beads.

Content required:
- **Inline context, not links.** Every bead description must be readable cold by an automated execute-bead worker that has no chat history and no open tabs.
- **Explicit in-scope file list.** Repo-relative paths. Bad: 'the auth module'. Good: 'cli/internal/auth/pipeline.go and cli/internal/auth/pipeline_test.go'.
- **Explicit do-NOT-touch scope negation** (non-goals). Prevents scope creep during agent execution.
- **Favor many small beads.** Rule of thumb: if a bead would touch more than ~3 files or more than one subsystem, decompose into child beads under an epic.
- **Acceptance is a command that passes, not a sentence.** 'X works' is not AC. 'cd cli && go test ./internal/foo/... passes and returns 0' is AC.
- **Implementations include tests.** The acceptance criteria must reference a test command.
- **Rollback/cleanup expectations** for partial changes — what 'leave it unclaimed' means in practice.
- **spec-id linkage** when a governing artifact exists (FEAT-*, SD-*, TD-*, ADR-*); omit when it doesn't.
- **CLI reference** at the end: ddx bead create/update/close/list/show/ready/blocked/dep/import/export/evidence.

Source material: existing /skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md (already contains much of this — especially the top-level "Execution-Ready" section added in an earlier refinement). Strip HELIX-specific rules: remove the mandatory 'helix' label and the phase:frame|design|test|build|deploy|iterate|review enumeration. Keep generic guidance ('label beads with area, kind, and any workflow tags your project uses').

In-scope files:
- skills/ddx/reference/beads.md (new)

Out-of-scope:
- skills/ddx/SKILL.md (separate bead ddx-bd852e86)
- skills/ddx/reference/{work,review,agents,status}.md (separate beads)","id":"ddx-0827da45","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:skills"],"parent":"ddx-f586b4fd","priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-011","status":"closed","title":"Create skills/ddx/reference/beads.md — execution-ready bead authoring","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:23:03.141018211Z"}

However there is no way to filter sessions by bead — you must know the session ID in advance or scroll through all recent sessions.

This bead adds --bead <id> filtering and a per-bead attempt summary view to the existing command.

## Required changes

### 1. --bead flag on ddx agent log (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)
When --bead <bead-id> is provided:
  - Scan all SessionEntry records in sessions.jsonl
  - Filter to entries where Correlation["bead_id"] == bead-id
  - Sort ascending by Timestamp (attempt order)
  - Display in attempt-history table format (see below)

No change to the default behavior (no --bead = show recent N sessions as today).

### 2. Attempt history table columns
ATTEMPT | STARTED | DURATION | HARNESS | MODEL | OUTCOME | TOKENS | COST | SESSION
- ATTEMPT: 1-based index in chronological order
- OUTCOME: merged / preserved / no-changes / error (from ExecuteBeadResult.Outcome via Correlation or result.json)
- SESSION: truncated session ID (8 chars) for use with ddx agent log <session-id>
- COST: formatted as /bin/zsh.0042 or 'local' if cost_usd == 0

### 3. Outcome resolution
SessionEntry does not directly store outcome — it stores exit_code and error. The full outcome is in .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/result.json (ExecuteBeadResult struct). When rendering the attempt table:
  - Use Correlation["attempt_id"] to find .ddx/executions/{attempt_id}/result.json
  - Read ExecuteBeadResult.Outcome from that file
  - Fall back to exit_code == 0 ? 'success' : 'error' if result.json is absent

### 4. Summary line
After the table, print a one-line summary:
  '<bead-id>: N attempts, M merged, K preserved, J errors. Last attempt: <elapsed> ago.'

### 5. --json passthrough
When --bead and --json are both set, emit the filtered SessionEntry array as JSON with an added outcome field resolved from result.json.

## Key files
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — newAgentLogCommand() lines 745-807, add --bead flag and filter path
- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry struct (lines 94-122), Correlation map field
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadResult struct (lines 30-64), artifact paths convention

Sources section cites at least two external works.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-17T22:37:54Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1512290","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:10:38.363323602Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-13ff86c6","depends_on_id":"ddx-5241bb19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:12:23Z"}],"description":"The existing library/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md is the strongest shipped persona content-wise, but it references DDx-internal paths that only exist in the DDx repo (docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md, cli/internal/agent/script.go, named DDx legacy offenders like orchTestGitOps/fakeExecuteBeadGit/gateTestGitOps/mockExecutor).

Rename to library/personas/test-engineer.md and generalize.

**CORE STANCES (load-bearing — preserve these):**
- Favor real e2e tests. At the e2e layer, do not mock anything — real infrastructure, real processes, real network where applicable. E2e tests that mock the database or the HTTP layer are unit tests lying about their scope.
- Stubs over mocks at the unit layer. Extract interfaces at domain boundaries; write in-memory stubs that return canned values set up by the test. Stubs do not record call sequences.
- Contract tests between in-memory stubs and real backends — same suite runs against both, proving they are interchangeable. This is the discipline that keeps stubs honest.
- Zero mocks in integration tests. Use real git (temp dir with scrubbed env), real filesystem (t.TempDir), real database (temp file / temp container), real HTTP (local test server). The only approved fake is at the AI-provider (or equivalent third-party-SDK) boundary.
- Behavior over implementation — assert on observable state, not on call counts or invocation sequences.
- Deterministic factory functions / fakers over static JSON fixtures.
- Test names describe behavior ("creates invoice when all line items valid"), not positions ("test_create_invoice_3").
- Testing pyramid as a *shape guide* (more unit than integration than e2e), not as mandated ratios.
- **Performance measurements require a baseline and clear boundaries.** Any claim that something is "fast", "scales", or "improved performance" must include: (a) a numeric baseline to compare against (prior version, prior run, published SLO), (b) an explicit boundary — what unit of work is being measured, what environment, what is excluded (cold-start, warmup, network latency, etc.), (c) reproducible harness so the measurement can be re-run. "It feels fast" and "benchmarks look good" are not performance claims.

**STRIP (cargo-cult testing dogma — do NOT ship):**
- Hard coverage percentages as universal rules (">= 80% overall", "100% on critical paths"). Replace with: "measure coverage where the project tracks it; coverage is a signal, not a target".
- Specific timing budgets as universal rules ("unit < 10ms", "integration < 100ms"). Replace with: "tests should be fast enough that developers run them; the specific threshold is a project concern".
- Specific pyramid percentages ("60% unit / 25% integration / 10% contract / 5% e2e") — keep the shape, drop the numbers.
- TDD as the only valid approach. Test-first is one workflow; test-alongside is another; test-after for a specific reason can be defensible. Do not call non-TDD "worst practice".
- Tests-for-the-sake-of-tests (mirror-of-spec tests that add no signal, trivial getters/setters).
- Mutation testing as a quality requirement. Mention as an optional signal, not a bar to clear.
- Snapshot tests without a maintenance plan.
- Retry loops around flaky tests as "improvement" — a retry loop hides a bug; fix the flake.
- Branch coverage vs line coverage dogma — say "coverage metrics matter less than whether tests catch real bugs".

**STRIP (DDx-internal specifics):**
- References to cli/internal/agent/script.go (replace with generic "a deterministic script-like fake at the AI-provider boundary")
- Named DDx legacy offenders (orchTestGitOps, fakeExecuteBeadGit, gateTestGitOps, mockExecutor) — these belong in a helix-test-engineer plugin persona, not this generic base
- Authoritative-sources frontmatter section citing docs/helix/concerns.md and HELIX concern.md
- Specific paths like cli/internal/bead, cli/internal/agent

Ground in external canon:
- Kent Beck - Test-Driven Development: By Example
- Martin Fowler - "Mocks Arent Stubs" (canonical reference for why call-sequence assertions are a code smell)
- Brendan Gregg - Systems Performance methodology (for the baseline + boundary stance on perf measurement)
- Anthropic Prompt Library (if a testing entry exists)
- TDD Guard from anthropics/skills repo (if the automated-test-first-enforcement pattern is worth mentioning)

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:145:{"acceptance":"- [ ] Option A, B, or C selected and documented in an ADR or design note under the ddx repo
- [ ] Implementation lands with migration path for existing .ddx/beads.jsonl files (no lost bead history)
- [ ] Concurrent writes from 2+ workers on the same machine produce a deterministic, semantically correct tracker state
- [ ] Concurrent writes from 2+ machines (via ddx server or shared remote) produce a deterministic, semantically correct tracker state
- [ ] Claim races are resolved by exactly one winner; losers see a structured 'already_claimed' error
- [ ] Crash recovery: a claimed bead whose worker died returns to unclaimed after the heartbeat timeout (existing mechanism continues to work)
- [ ] Existing beads.jsonl format continues to work as a read-only snapshot for offline tooling (jq queries, etc.)
- [ ] Unit + integration tests covering the concurrency scenarios above
- [ ] Migration test: apply the upgrade to axon's current beads.jsonl (748+ beads on sindri side, 30+ new on eitri side) and verify no semantic loss","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:20:46.447948869Z","description":"Fourth child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. .ddx/beads.jsonl is a git-tracked mutable ledger. Multiple writers on different machines or in parallel worker processes produce divergent files that require manual textual merge. git merge can sometimes append-merge JSONL cleanly if each side touches disjoint lines, but when both sides touch the same bead (claim / close / add-event), the merge is semantically broken even when git reports it as resolved.

## Evidence

On axon, eitri and sindri both wrote to beads.jsonl independently for 14 days. Local has 134 commits touching the tracker; origin has 336. When I tried to rebase my planning commit onto origin, git reported conflicts in .ddx/beads.jsonl — and these conflicts would have been 'resolved' by git's textual merge logic even when the semantic outcome is wrong (e.g. two machines claiming the same bead with different claimed-machine values).

The existing execute-loop help text says:

    By default execute-loop submits to the running ddx server as a background
    worker and returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current
    process.

So a ddx server EXISTS and is supposed to coordinate. But .ddx/beads.jsonl is still the ledger, which means the server is not the source of truth — it's just a submission surface. The git-tracked file is authoritative, and that's what's racing.

## Options (pick one during implementation)

**A. Server-authoritative tracker.** When the ddx server is running, it owns beads.jsonl. All reads and writes go through the server's API. The git-tracked file becomes a periodic snapshot for backup/offline use, not the source of truth. Workers on multiple machines talk to a shared server.

**B. CRDT append-only log.** Replace the current representation with an append-only log keyed by (machine_id, bead_id, sequence, timestamp). Each writer appends to its own partition; readers compute the effective state by merging partitions with a conflict-resolution rule (last-writer-wins per (bead_id, field), or better). git textual merge of the partitioned log is always safe because writers never share lines.

**C. Per-bead lock files with atomic rename.** Each bead mutation acquires a lock file (beads/<id>.lock), writes beads/<id>.json atomically via rename, releases the lock. The JSONL aggregate is regenerated from per-bead files. Git tracks per-bead files, not the aggregate. Safe across local parallelism but not across machines without network coordination.

**Recommendation**: A is the cleanest if the ddx server is already deployed as authoritative infrastructure. B is the best fit for the current decentralized model. C is a stopgap that solves intra-machine parallelism but not multi-machine drift.

## Scope note

Do NOT conflate this bead with the fetch/push/rebase triad (ddx-bd42f640, ddx-83ec1430, ddx-6aa50e57). Those three fix the git-level divergence; this bead fixes the tracker-level divergence. Both are needed — the triad without tracker safety would still produce semantically-conflicting beads.jsonl merges even when the underlying git history is linear.

## Test cases

- Two workers on the same machine claim different beads concurrently: both claims persist correctly
- Two workers on different machines claim different beads concurrently: both claims persist correctly (requires network coordination)
- Two workers attempt to claim the same bead: one wins, the other sees 'already claimed' and picks a different one
- Recovery from crash between claim and CloseWithEvidence: the bead returns to unclaimed after a heartbeat timeout
- Tracker state is deterministic from any valid ordering of concurrent writes (CRDT option)","id":"ddx-2791dc4f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["bug","execute-loop","tracker","concurrency"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-820c201","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: .ddx/beads.jsonl concurrent-write safety","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:45:05.880884507Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:161:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, I expect the Sessions page to reflect reality — if I ran `ddx work` today, today's sessions appear within seconds. And I expect the feed not to silently break again the next time the agent execution path is refactored.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Session-index source of truth.** A single writer (invoked from `service.Execute` or a thin wrapper that every production agent-execution path calls) appends one line per completed agent invocation to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl`. Fields are pointer-only: id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath (relative), nativeLogRef (relative). No prompt or response bodies embedded.

2. **Single-call-site guarantee.** Unit test enumerates every production code path that executes an agent (execute-bead, execute-loop, `ddx agent run`, quorum, replay if still present) and asserts each appends exactly one index row. If a new path is added later without wiring this up, the test fails.

3. **Visibility restored.** GraphQL `sessions` query reads `sessions.index.jsonl`. Running `ddx work` (or any agent-execution path) against a fixture project causes the new session to appear in a GraphQL query within 2 seconds of completion. E2E test demonstrates this.

4. **Body lazy-load.** A new resolver `session(id: ID!) { prompt response stderr }` loads the heavy fields on demand from the execution bundle (`.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/`) or the agent-log path, without reading every session in the index.

5. **Legacy migration.** A one-shot command (e.g. `ddx agent log reindex`) seeds the index from the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (397 MB corpus on the current repo), preserving historical sessions. Integration test uses a fixture `sessions.jsonl` and asserts the index has one row per legacy entry with all pointer fields populated.

6. **Deprecated-file behavior.** After migration, the legacy `sessions.jsonl` is not read by the server. `ddx agent usage` and related CLI commands (`cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:197,246,348`, `cli/cmd/status.go:179`) are updated to read from the index too, verified by their existing tests.

7. **Perf sanity.** The GraphQL `sessions` list resolves in ≤100ms p95 on a fixture with 10,000 index entries; current behavior times out or OOMs. Benchmark captured in bead notes as baseline + after-fix.

8. **UI note.** If there is a gap in the index (e.g. the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap on the current repo, assuming backfill from executions/agent-logs is not feasible), the Sessions page surfaces a single-line banner "No sessions recorded between X and Y" so the gap is not silently invisible. If backfill is feasible, do it instead and skip the banner.

9. **Playwright e2e.**
   - Seeds a fixture project with `.ddx/executions/` bundles from both before and after a simulated gap.
   - Runs the migration command.
   - Opens `/sessions`, asserts the most recent session timestamp matches the latest bundle, asserts a clicked row lazy-loads prompt/response from the bundle rather than from an embedded payload.
   - Asserts the feed updates live (within 2s) after a new simulated session is written.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"d733f7f20828708c5179f0ceff9462eaa34654eb","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:22:41.990684692Z","description":"## Observed

The Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.

## Root cause

The Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.

The only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:

- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`
- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`
- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`

Production execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.

## Secondary issue (same fix should address)

`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).

## Proposed direction

Do not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:

1. **Decide the source of truth for "a session".** Current candidates on disk:
   - `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.
   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.
   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).
   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.

2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.

3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.

4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.

5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).

## Out of scope

- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.
- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.","events_attachment":"ddx-2ceb02fa/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model "minimax/minimax-m2.7" for harness "gemini"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"feature":"FEAT-010","id":"ddx-2ceb02fa","issue_type":"bug","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","feat-006","perf","observability"],"notes":"## 2026-04-22 — User-directed amendment: shard the index

Per user feedback: a single `sessions.index.jsonl` just replays the same problem at smaller scale. The aggregate needs to be split.

### Revised storage shape

- Directory: `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/` (per-project, under each registered project's `.ddx`).
- Inside: **monthly shards** named `sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`.
- Each shard is pointer-only, same fields as the previously-proposed index (id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath, nativeLogRef).
- Writer resolves the target shard from `time.Now().UTC()` at append time and creates the shard file lazily on first write of the month.
- Heavy bodies (prompt, response, stderr) stay where they already live — `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/` and agent-log streams — and are lazy-loaded by the `session(id:)` resolver.

### Why monthly, not daily / size-based / SQLite

- **Daily** — produces tens of thousands of files over the project lifetime; ReadDir becomes the cost.
- **Size-based** — requires coordinating rotation with the append; race-prone and hard to test.
- **SQLite** — overkill for first pass; revisit if a single monthly shard grows past tens of MB.
- **Monthly** — ≤12 shards/year, human-readable filenames, date-range queries map trivially to shard selection, bounded shard size in practice.

### Reader behavior

- Default Sessions query loads the **current + previous shard** (covers "recent activity" for any current date).
- Date-range queries map the range to a shard set and read only those.
- Pagination beyond the default window triggers older-shard loads on demand.
- No shard-coordination lock needed: each month's file is append-only and only the current-month shard is actively written.

### Legacy migration

- `ddx agent log reindex` reads the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` once and splits by each entry's `timestamp` field into the appropriate monthly shards under `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/`.
- After migration, the legacy file is renamed `sessions.jsonl.legacy` (not deleted) and never read again by the server. Hand-removal is the user's call.
- Idempotent: re-running the command merges without duplicating (index row dedup by `id`).

### Revised acceptance criteria (supersedes criteria 1, 5, 6, 7 on original description)

1. **Sharded index writer.** Single shared call site (from `service.Execute` or equivalent wrapper) appends one line to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, computing the shard from `time.Now().UTC()`. Test: write across a simulated month boundary and assert two shards exist with the correct split.

5. **Legacy migration shards too.** `ddx agent log reindex` splits `sessions.jsonl` into monthly shards by entry timestamp. Integration test uses a fixture with entries across 3 distinct months and asserts 3 shards are produced with correct contents and no duplicates on re-run.

6. **Server reads the shard directory.** `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` reads `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/*.jsonl`, respects any date-range arg, and loads only the shards that intersect the range. Unit test seeds 6 monthly shards and asserts a 2-month query reads exactly 2 files.

7. **Perf on sharded index.** Default Sessions view (current + previous shard) resolves in ≤50ms p95 on a fixture with 12 monthly shards × 10k entries each (120k total). Capture baseline (single-file read of same corpus) vs sharded in bead notes.

(Criteria 2, 3, 4, 8, 9 from the original acceptance list remain as written.)

### Scope clarifier

This amendment does not change the single-call-site writer guarantee or the lazy-body resolver — those remain the load-bearing ideas. The amendment is specifically about **how the pointer index is physically stored**.
## 2026-04-23 — Implementation benchmark and evidence

Baseline: `go test ./internal/agent -run '^$' -bench 'Benchmark(ReadSessionIndexDefaultWindow|LegacySessionsJSONLFullScan)$' -benchtime=1x` measured legacy single-file full scan/sort of 120k legacy-shaped rows with 2KB embedded responses at `BenchmarkLegacySessionsJSONLFullScan-12 1 1731705957 ns/op`.

After fix: the same command measured the sharded default-window reader at `BenchmarkReadSessionIndexDefaultWindow-12 1 8103661 ns/op` for 12 monthly shards x 10k rows each (120k total), with the default query reading only the current + previous shard window. Environment footer: linux/arm64, Go go1.26.2.

The server currently registers ~50 routes under /api/... without project scoping. FEAT-002 requires /api/projects/{project}/... as canonical. Three worker routes already use this pattern (handleProjectWorkerList, handleProjectWorkerShow, handleProjectWorkerProgress) — follow that pattern.

## Implementation approach
1. Create a projectMiddleware (or helper function) that:
   - Extracts {project} from the URL path
   - Resolves it via GetProjectByID() or GetProjectByPath()
   - Injects the resolved ProjectEntry into request context
   - Returns 404 if project not found
2. Register all document, bead, and doc-graph routes under /api/projects/{project}/...
3. Keep legacy /api/... routes as singleton compatibility aliases that auto-resolve to the single registered project (error if >1 project registered without scoping)
4. Add test(s) that register two projects, verify scoped routes resolve correctly, and verify legacy routes work with one project

- cli/internal/server/server.go — route registration, middleware
- cli/internal/server/handlers.go (or individual handler files) — extract project from context instead of hardcoding
- cli/internal/server/server_test.go — new project-scoped routing tests

## Files NOT to touch
- MCP tools (separate bead)
- GraphQL schema (already has beadsByProject, workersByProject)
- Frontend routes","id":"ddx-36d2775f","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:build","area:api","kind:implementation"],"parent":"hx-bed139d3","priority":0,"status":"closed","title":"Add project-scoped route middleware and migrate document/bead/graph routes","updated_at":"2026-04-16T20:27:23.846100615Z"}

3. Token-budget guard: a unit test asserts that when a `<bead-review>` prompt would exceed 150k tokens even after the exclusion, the builder elides to a summary stub (per suggested fix #2) and logs a warning rather than emitting the full content.

4. 5. 6. Existing execution evidence continues to satisfy auditability: either still tracked but excluded from review diffs, or moved to a dedicated ref / ignored store per the chosen fix. `ddx agent metrics` and `ddx bead metrics` still surface per-attempt usage/tokens/cost from the evidence bundle after the fix.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T00:13:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1551897","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:08:23.667089738Z","description":"## Summary

On retry, DDx synthesizes a <bead-review> prompt whose <diff> section is the output of `git show <resultRev>`. When the prior attempt's landing sequence appended an evidence commit of the form `chore: add execution evidence [<attempt_id>]` that added `.ddx/executions/<attempt_id>/embedded/agent-*.jsonl` (full per-event NDJSON session log) as a tracked file, `resultRev` is that evidence commit's SHA (because `landEvidence` sets `result.NewTip = sha` and the orchestrator copies `land.NewTip` into `res.ResultRev`). `git show` of the evidence commit emits the entire session log diff with a `+` prefix per line. Session logs are 5-50k lines; the retry prompt exceeds every public context window.

## Category / severity / surface

- Category: correctness / resource-exhaustion
- Severity: high
- Surface: `ddx agent execute-bead` retry path triggered by `kind:review-malfunction` (and any other flow that re-synthesizes a <bead-review> prompt from a git diff/show since base_rev).

## Full bug description (verbatim)

On retry, DDx synthesizes a <bead-review> prompt whose <diff> section is the raw `git diff <base_rev>..<result_rev>` of the prior attempt. The prior attempt's landing sequence includes an evidence commit of the form:

    chore: add execution evidence [<attempt_id>]

that adds `.ddx/executions/<attempt_id>/embedded/agent-*.jsonl` — the full per-event NDJSON session log of the prior attempt — as a tracked file. Because that file is *added* in the diff range, the diff includes it in full with a `+` prefix on every line. Session logs are routinely 5-50k lines. One reasonable fix iteration is enough to push the retry prompt past 1-2 million tokens, which no currently supported model can accept — the provider streams back an n_keep > n_ctx error and the retry crashes without a request round-trip.

This is a self-poisoning loop: the "evidence" DDx commits to make attempts auditable is the exact artifact that makes the next attempt undeliverable.

## Reproduction (observed live 2026-04-17 in nexiq)

1. Bead `hx-dd4ab35f` claimed by execute-loop worker (harness=agent, model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b, provider=vidar).
2. Attempt `20260417T211600-d6a7dee6` succeeded and landed two commits: `cb5e733` (implementation + regression test [hx-dd4ab35f]) and `9a006dd` (chore: add execution evidence [20260417T211600-], adds `.ddx/executions/20260417T211600-d6a7dee6/embedded/agent-1776460560227794836.jsonl`, +20,623 lines).
3. Post-merge review emitted `BLOCK` without a rationale; DDx recorded `kind:review-malfunction` and reopened the bead for retry.
4. Retry `20260417T212928-27f9054a` synthesized `.ddx/executions/20260417T212928-27f9054a/prompt.md` of 6,307,997 bytes; `<bead-review>` block = 6,307,969 bytes; 99.9998% is the diff of the prior evidence commit, 99%+ of that diff is the session log file. Rest of <bead-review> (title/description/acceptance/labels/governing) is ~28 bytes.
5. lmstudio responded: "n_keep: 2,274,074 >= n_ctx: 196,608". Worker recorded failed attempt with zero usage. Same pattern on vidar (n_keep: 1,169,147) and bragi (n_keep: 2,274,074), all running qwen3.6-35b-a3b at max_context_length 262144. Provider/model is irrelevant — prompt exceeds every public context window.

## DDx code-path verification (Claude investigation 2026-04-17)

Relevant files and line ranges in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx:

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:553-561` — `DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow(rev)` runs `git -C <projectRoot> show <rev>` with no pathspec. Output is injected raw into `<diff>` by `BuildReviewPrompt` at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:383-384`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:427-431` — reviewer fetches `diff := r.gitShow(resultRev)` then builds the prompt.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:540-555` (`landEvidence`) — creates `chore: add execution evidence [<short>]` and sets `result.NewTip = sha`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:428-434` — `RealLandingGitOps.StageDir` runs `git add -- <relPath>` on the entire `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` directory with no exclusions for `embedded/`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:250-251` — after Land, `res.ResultRev = land.NewTip`, so `ResultRev` ends up pointing at the evidence commit. The reviewer then runs `git show <evidence-commit>`, which is literally the dump of the session log.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1135-1164` (`VerifyCleanWorktree`) — also stages the evidence dir via `git add -- <evidenceDir>` and commits `chore: add execution evidence [<short>]`; same no-exclusion pattern.
- `cli/cmd/bead_review.go:162-171` (`beadReviewGitShow`) — mirror of the same bug in the standalone `ddx bead review` command.

Confirmation the bug reporter's claim about `:(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded`:
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:269-283` (`SynthesizeCommit`) — the exclusion pathspec IS present here; the bug is that the evidence-landing commit path (`StageDir` + `VerifyCleanWorktree`) and the review path (`git show <rev>`) do not share this exclusion.
- `cli/cmd/init.go:19-46` — the gitignore rules DO include `.ddx/executions/*/embedded/`, but `git add -- <dir>` on pre-existing or explicitly-added paths can still stage, and older workspaces may pre-date this gitignore (matches nexiq's scenario where the evidence commit landed the session log).

## Deviation from bug description (minor)

The bug text describes the diff as `git diff <base_rev>..<result_rev>`. The code actually does `git show <resultRev>` (single-rev show). The self-poisoning mechanism is functionally identical: when `resultRev` points at the evidence commit (`landEvidence` + `res.ResultRev = land.NewTip`), `git show` emits the entire `+`-prefixed session log. All suggested fixes still apply.

## Suggested fixes (ascending scope)

1. Exclude execution-evidence paths from bead-review diffs by passing a pathspec to the `git show`/`git diff` call in the prompt builder: `-- . ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded/*' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/prompt.md' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/usage.json'`. The same exclusion already exists at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:269-283` for `SynthesizeCommit`; replicate it in `DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow` (`execute_bead_review.go:553-561`), `beadReviewGitShow` (`cli/cmd/bead_review.go:162-171`), `landEvidence` → `StageDir` (`execute_bead_land.go:428-434,540-555`), and `VerifyCleanWorktree` (`execute_bead.go:1135-1164`).
2. Substitute, don't reproduce. When a tracked-file addition exceeds a byte/line budget in the diff, replace it with a one-line stub such as: `+++ b/.ddx/executions/.../agent-*.jsonl  (+20,623 lines of session evidence — omitted from review prompt)`.
3. Stop committing raw session logs as tracked evidence. Move `.ddx/executions/*/embedded/*.jsonl` to a gitignored store, or a separate `refs/ddx/evidence/*` ref analogous to `refs/ddx/iterations/*`.
4. Cap the <bead-review> prompt size with a hard budget (e.g., 150k tokens). Elide to summary + log warning when exceeded.

(1) is the minimal surgical fix; (3) is the correct long-term fix.

Both reviews flagged the same missing concept: routing currently scores candidates on cost class, quota, and historical success rate — but does NOT gate on whether the resolved route can actually execute the requested work. Concrete failure modes this lets through:

1. **Context-window overflow.** Bead prompt is 80k tokens. Fuzzy match picks a 32k-context model on the pinned provider. Routing 'succeeds'. Dispatch fails mid-stream with a provider 400 or truncated output.

2. **Missing tool-calling support.** Some omlx flavors or older local servers don't implement /v1/chat/completions with tools. Routing picks them via fuzzy match. Dispatch fails with 'tools not supported' or silently returns a no-tool response that the execute-bead loop can't use.

3. **Missing structured-output support.** Some provider/model combos don't implement JSON-mode / tool-use-required. If the bead prompt relies on it, routing-level blindness means the failure only surfaces at dispatch.

4. **Effort / permissions.** --effort high or --permissions unrestricted may not be honored by every harness+provider combination. Routing should reject or downgrade rather than dispatch and fail.

Codex: 'routing currently scores mostly on cost/quota/history, not whether a provider/model can satisfy tool calling, effort controls, permissions, or context-window needs.'
Opus: 'If the bead prompt is 80k tokens and the fuzzy-matched model is 32k, routing succeeds and dispatch fails mid-stream. Omlx flavor may not support tools at all.'

## Out of scope for this bead

This bead tracks the CONCEPT as a sibling of the main autorouting epic, but a full capability-constraint scoring system is a larger change than the current Phase-1 scope. Per opus: 'Scope this out of the epic explicitly (don't expand), but add a sibling bead so it's tracked.'

Split out into sub-scope bites is TBD:

1. **Context-window gating** — smallest useful slice. RouteRequest gains an optional EstimatedPromptTokens field; evaluateCandidate rejects (or penalizes) candidates whose concrete_model's known context window is below that. Uses v0.3.11's LookupModelLimits + catalog ContextWindow fallback.

2. **Tool-calling support gating** — needs per-provider / per-flavor capability metadata. Likely populated from discovery (/v1/models/status) + hand-curated capability table for harnesses.

3. **Effort / permissions gating** — mostly already in place for harnesses (Harness.EffortFlag checked at routing.go:rejectIfNotViable) but provider-specific gating (e.g., 'openrouter doesn't honor effort on non-reasoning models') is not.

4. **Structured-output support** — probably lowest priority; most reasoning-tool-capable models also do structured output.

This bead's AC is to file the sub-beads (smaller, focused) and close THIS bead once the sub-beads exist. The epic ddx-2d974641 does NOT depend on those sub-beads for its exit gate — context-window etc. are a Phase 2 concern. The epic is about correct resolution; capability gating is about preventing resolved-but-unrunnable routes.

## Files touched (by sub-beads, not this one)

- cli/internal/agent/types.go — CandidatePlan capability fields, RouteRequest capability hints
- cli/internal/agent/routing.go — capability checks in rejectIfNotViable + scoring

Every agent that adopts this persona produces mock-heavy tests that violate the concern's rules. This is how DDx accumulated `orchTestGitOps`, `fakeExecuteBeadGit`, `gateTestGitOps`, `TestConcurrentWorkersNoMergeRace`, `mockExecutor`, and every other test-that-lies-about-its-coverage pattern the opus test plan review surfaced.

## Provenance

- Persona authored 2025-09-15 in commit a4cc8ac as part of FEAT-011 persona system build
- Testing concern adopted in DDx 2026-04-05 in commit b711ac8 (`Add testing concern`)
- The persona has been silently contradicting the concern for 9 days with no audit
- Personas are a DDx-native concept (HELIX has no personas/ directory in workflows/); this is DDx's problem to fix

## The core principle

**Mocks are a worst practice and should be avoided in 99% of cases.** The right pattern, always, is: extract an interface at your domain boundary, write an in-memory stub implementation of that interface, and write a contract test suite that runs against BOTH the stub and the real backend. When both pass the same suite they are proven interchangeable. This exposes hidden assumptions, keeps stubs honest, and produces tests that assert behavior instead of call sequences.

Mocks — defined specifically as call-recording test doubles that assert method X was called Y times with args Z — test implementation instead of behavior. They break when internals change without user-visible consequences. They give false confidence: a passing mock test proves nothing about the system's actual behavior because the thing under test never interacts with a real dependency. Most notoriously in DDx: mock git tests have proven zero merge-race, rebase-conflict, or serialization invariants despite claiming to.

The 1% of cases where mocks are acceptable: third-party SDK boundaries where a stub is infeasible (payment processors with real-world-only callbacks, email delivery, SMS, external auth providers). Even there, prefer contract tests against a sandbox environment when one exists.

## Offending sections

library/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md lines 90-94 (Unit Tests):

```
### Unit Tests
- Test individual functions/methods in isolation
- Mock all external dependencies
- Focus on business logic
- Use test doubles (mocks, stubs, spies)
```

`Mock all external dependencies` is absolute and wrong. The HELIX testing concern says:

> Prefer stubs (canned return values) over mocks (call-sequence assertions). Stubs test behavior; mocks test implementation.
> Use real implementations where practical: real databases via testcontainers or Docker Compose, real HTTP via local servers, real file systems via temp directories.
> Mock only at true system boundaries: third-party APIs, payment processors, email services, external auth providers.
> Never mock the thing you are testing.

`Use test doubles (mocks, stubs, spies)` lists mocks alongside stubs as equivalent. They are not equivalent. Mocks assert call sequences (implementation detail). Stubs return canned values (behavior contract). The persona should never put them on the same line.

library/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md lines 96-100 (Integration Tests):

```
### Integration Tests
- Test component interactions
- Use real implementations where possible
- Test database operations
- Verify API contracts
```

`Use real implementations where possible` is weakly worded. DDx's project override in concerns.md states:

> Integration tests: zero mocks. No exceptions. Integration tests exercise real production implementations of every dependency in the graph — real git (t.TempDir() + scrubbed GIT_* env), real filesystem, real bead store, real coordinator, real server, real agent harness (the script harness is the only approved fake for the AI-provider boundary, and it does not mock — it performs real filesystem and git operations deterministically). A test that substitutes any mock for a production component is not an integration test. It is a unit test that is lying about its coverage.

The persona must echo this in absolute language, not "where possible."

## What to change

### Rewrite the Unit Tests section

Replace lines 90-94 with guidance that matches the HELIX concern and DDx override exactly. Required content:

- The code under test depends on interfaces at domain boundaries. Extract the interface if it does not exist.
- Isolate the code under test with in-memory STUB implementations of those interfaces. Stubs return canned values the test sets up. They never record call sequences.
- Every stub must be backed by a contract test suite that the real implementation ALSO passes. Same suite, both implementations. That proves they are interchangeable.
- Never use testify/mock, gomock, mockery-generated interfaces for assertion purposes, or any call-sequence assertion library. Assert on behavior — what did the code do to observable state? — not on call counts.
- Never mock git, filesystem, bead store, database, queue, or any first-party infrastructure — use real implementations in temp directories.
- The only approved "fake" in DDx unit tests is an in-memory implementation of a first-party interface you own.
- Mocks are a worst practice. Avoid them in 99% of cases in favor of extracted interfaces with in-memory stubs. The 1% exception is third-party SDK boundaries where a stub is infeasible.

### Rewrite the Integration Tests section

Replace lines 96-100 with:

- Integration tests use zero mocks. Period. No exceptions.
- Real database in a temp dir, real filesystem via t.TempDir(), real git via git init in a temp dir with scrubbed GIT_* env, real HTTP via a local test server, real queues, real bead store, real coordinator, real server.
- The only approved fake in the DDx suite is the `script` harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) as the AI-provider boundary replacement. It is not a mock — it performs real filesystem and git operations deterministically against the worktree.
- A test that substitutes any mock for a production component is not an integration test. It is a unit test that is lying about its coverage.
- Never mock the thing you are testing. A coordinator test that substitutes a fake LandingGitOps is mocking the thing under test; use RealLandingGitOps against a real git repo.

### Add a Contract Tests section

The persona currently has a Contract Tests section at lines 102-106. Rewrite it to cover the "stub must pass the real implementation's contract suite" pattern, not just "APIs meet specifications." Example structure:

```go
// contract_test.go — runs the same suite against both implementations
func TestBeadStoreContract(t *testing.T) {
    cases := []struct {
        name  string
        build func(t *testing.T) Store
    }{
        {"memory", func(t *testing.T) Store { return NewMemoryStore() }},
        {"file",   func(t *testing.T) Store { return NewFileStore(t.TempDir()) }},
    }
    for _, c := range cases {
        t.Run(c.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            s := c.build(t)
            t.Run("CreateAndGet", func(t *testing.T) { /* ... */ })
            t.Run("UpdateLosesNoFields", func(t *testing.T) { /* ... */ })
            t.Run("ConcurrentWritersSerialized", func(t *testing.T) { /* ... */ })
        })
    }
}
```

If the stub passes and the real backend fails, the backend is broken or the stub is lying. Either way the contract suite exposes the drift immediately. This is how DDx keeps stubs honest.

### Add a Drift Signals subsection

Mirror the HELIX testing concern's drift signals and add DDx-specific anti-patterns. Any agent reviewing code that contains these should reject or refactor:

- `testify/mock` imports in new test files
- `gomock` generated mocks for first-party interfaces
- Structs named `*Mock*`, `fake*Git`, `fake*Store`, `orchTest*GitOps`, `gateTest*GitOps` that implement a first-party interface for assertion purposes (in-memory stubs for first-party interfaces are OK and encouraged, as long as they are real implementations, not call recorders)
- Per-goroutine mock git in concurrency tests (the shared-writer invariant is not being tested)
- test.skip, .skip(), @Ignore, @Disabled without a linked issue
- Mock-heavy tests that assert call order rather than observable state
- Static JSON fixture files (use factory functions with fakers instead)
- Tests named `test1`, `testHelper`, `it works` (rename to describe behavior)
- `expect(true).toBe(true)` or tautological assertions
- Commented-out tests (git has the history; delete them)
- Retry loop around a flaky test (fix the flake)

### Add citations to authoritative sources

The persona must explicitly cite:

- **HELIX testing concern**: `workflows/concerns/testing/concern.md` in HELIX (or `.ddx/plugins/helix/workflows/concerns/testing/concern.md` when installed) — the upstream authority for the stubs-over-mocks rule.
- **DDx concerns.md**: `docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md` — the DDx-specific application of the rule, including the zero-mocks-in-integration-tests override.
- **Script harness**: `cli/internal/agent/script.go` — the canonical deterministic fake agent.
- **Integration test reference**: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go` — the reference pattern that every new integration test should follow.

### Update the installed copy

library/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md is the source of truth. The installed copy at .ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md must also be updated so downstream projects (HELIX and others) that depend on the DDx plugin pick up the fix. Either (a) update both files in the same commit and let the install process pick up the new source the next time it runs, or (b) run the library sync mechanism as part of the bead work.

## Why this matters

Every test in DDx's history that was written by an agent following this persona produced mock-heavy code. Those tests passed CI, appeared to cover invariants, and silently proved nothing. The alpha14 → alpha16 session has been the first time anyone audited them end-to-end and discovered the drift. Without this fix, the next agent adopting the persona will reproduce the same broken pattern.

The cost of fixing the persona is hours. The cost of NOT fixing it is weeks of debugging "why doesn't the land coordinator work" when the real answer is "because every test that 'proved' the old merge path worked was a lie."","id":"ddx-482fad2f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:library","area:docs","area:testing","kind:correctness"],"priority":1,"session_id":"eb-51e0142","status":"closed","title":"test-engineer-tdd persona contradicts testing concern (promotes mocks)","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:43:14.958570875Z"}

## Operating principles

1. Cheap models do the work. Local (vidar-coder / qwen3-coder-next / other LM Studio hosts), then budget-tier cloud (claude haiku / gpt-5.4-mini), then standard (claude sonnet / gpt-5.4), then smart (claude opus / gpt-5.4 high-effort). Start cheap; escalate on failure.
2. Strong models review. Every closed bead gets an opus review against its acceptance. Rejected reviews reopen with specific fixes.
3. Deterministic fixes are the top rung of the ladder. When LLMs keep failing on a mechanical task, apply the fix manually; capture as input for routing policy.
4. Measurement feeds optimization. Track per-bead which model attempted, how long, whether it passed review, what failure mode if any.

## Existing bead tie-ins (helix review-loop epic from parallel agent)

- ddx-a317208c exec: post-merge review agent on-by-default in execute-loop
- ddx-dc231144 exec: tier-based auto-escalation driven by resource discovery
- ddx-fdf031c9 exec: ddx bead review <id> — template a review prompt
- ddx-44084b3e exec: ddx bead reopen <id> — atomic review-driven reopen
- ddx-518b2690 exec: ddx agent run --output text — plain result extraction

## Existing bead tie-ins (mine)

- ddx-bd0691fe Epic: surface ddx-agent routing visibility (providers/models/check/route-status commands — prereq for routing policy)
- ddx-bfc1aeaf Visibility 8: capture routing decisions in bead evidence (kind:routing) — feeds the measurement database

## New child beads (to be filed under this epic)

The beads below are scope for this epic. File as children when we are ready to work each one. NOT pre-filed to avoid blocking the current Svelte+GraphQL migration.

1. Per-bead cost aggregation: extend bead evidence schema to record tokens-in/tokens-out/cost-usd per attempt; add ddx bead metrics aggregate command that rolls up per-bead totals.
2. Ladder success-rate analytics: ddx agent metrics tier-success — query last N closed beads, report success rate at each tier (vidar-coder, claude-sonnet, claude-opus, deterministic-fix).
3. Prompt-context pre-filter: shrink the prompt for cheap-tier attempts — omit the full plan doc and include only the bead description + acceptance + 2 most relevant files.
4. Failure-mode taxonomy: document the known failure modes (vidar long-context hang, read-loop stall, ship-unbuilt-code, opus-shape-miss, etc.) as bead evidence kinds so measurement can aggregate by mode.
5. Review-rejection telemetry: aggregate opus-review outcomes (APPROVE / APPROVE_WITH_EDITS / REJECT) per originating tier so we know where the shape gaps are.
6. Dashboard page for the metrics (requires Svelte migration Stage 4 to land first).

## Why this is captured as an epic not a memory-only goal

Memory is read; epics are tracked. Future sessions should see this in ddx bead list and be able to file child beads against it as we discover new measurement needs or optimization opportunities. ## Goals
- Show configured providers/harnesses, effective routing availability, auth/health state, and current quota or headroom when known
- Show utilization and performance signals already normalized by DDx: token usage, cost when available, recent latency/success, burn estimates, and freshness timestamps
- Support search/sort/filter/reporting across provider, model, profile, status, and time window
- Distinguish provider-native facts from DDx-derived estimates and from unknown values

## Required spec work
- Update FEAT-002, FEAT-008, and FEAT-014 so the dashboard and its backing read model are explicitly governed
- Define the API/read-model fields exposed to UI consumers and reporting surfaces
- Clarify how live provider availability, cached quota snapshots, and historical usage are presented without inventing certainty

- Define sample/fixture data needed to Playwright-test the dashboard states
- Define how the dashboard relates to worker progress, execution history, and routing diagnostics without collapsing them into one page","events_attachment":"ddx-573c6141/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-573c6141","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:planning","kind:architecture","area:agent","area:api","area:ui","area:docs"],"notes":"Closed as accidental duplicate created during tracker-context bug reproduction; canonical follow-on bead is ddx-359706d2.","parent":"ddx-8b6cd40e","priority":0,"spec-id":"FEAT-008","status":"closed","title":"Design server agent dashboard for provider availability and utilization","updated_at":"2026-04-10T22:21:01.051575607Z"}

- [ ] Docs: update TD-010 (or add TD-011) with the mirror design and operator runbook","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T06:17:17Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"d810ddff35013426b0e3ec92f4e1ad1fcb45c777","created_at":"2026-04-15T18:59:41.811906799Z","description":"Execution bundles under .ddx/executions/<attempt>/ are the primary forensic record of what DDx automation did, but the most valuable part — the per-iteration agent trace at embedded/agent-*.jsonl — cannot reasonably be checked in. Individual traces can exceed 50 MB; a busy automation day produces hundreds of MB. Axon hit this on 2026-04-15 when GitHub warned about a 50.18 MB embedded trace file, and embedded/ is now gitignored per-repo.

We still want that level of detail for *every* run, for post-mortems, routing/model analysis, and training-data curation. We need a configurable out-of-band mirror.

## Proposed shape

1. **Archive target** configured via `.ddx/config.yaml` (and/or env var):
   - `executions.mirror.kind: local | s3 | gcs | http`
   - `executions.mirror.path: s3://bucket/ddx-executions/{project}/{attempt_id}/` (template with `{project}`, `{attempt_id}`, `{date}`, `{bead_id}`)
   - `executions.mirror.include: [manifest, prompt, result, usage, checks, embedded]` (allow excluding embedded for bandwidth-sensitive setups, but default = everything)
   - `executions.mirror.async: true` (upload in background after execute-bead returns so it never blocks the hot path)

2. **When to mirror**: at execute-bead finalization, after result.json is written. On failure, retry async with bounded attempts; log failures to `.ddx/agent-logs/mirror.log` but do not fail the bead.

3. **Index**: each mirrored bundle gets a pointer row appended to a local `.ddx/executions/mirror-index.jsonl` (attempt_id, bead_id, mirror_uri, uploaded_at, byte_size). This gives analysts a quick `jq` lookup without walking the remote store.

4. **Retrieval**: `ddx agent executions fetch <attempt_id>` pulls a bundle back to .ddx/executions/<attempt_id>/ for local inspection.

5. **GC**: optional local retention policy (`executions.retain_days`) that prunes old local bundles while the mirror keeps the full history.

## Non-goals

- Not a full audit trail service — just a mirror of DDx's existing bundles.
- Not a replacement for git-tracked manifest/prompt/result/usage summaries — those stay checked in.
- Not a distributed log aggregator — one-shot copy per bundle, no streaming.

## Why now

Axon's ADR-018 session produced ~85 MB of execution artifacts in a single day; only 1.5 MB of that is useful as checked-in summary. Without a mirror, every project using DDx execute-bead faces the same tension: either gitignore the traces and lose the forensic data, or commit them and bloat the repo forever.","events_attachment":"ddx-5930ed71/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-18T06:37:17.253079303Z","execute-loop-last-detail":"execution_failed","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-retry-after":"","id":"ddx-5930ed71","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","area:agent","area:observability","area:storage","kind:feature"],"owner":"erik","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-312c9be3","status":"closed","title":"execution-bundle archive: configurable mirror for .ddx/executions/ (including embedded traces)","updated_at":"2026-04-18T06:37:50.507998511Z"}

Today, when execute-loop dispatches a coarse bead (an unsplit epic) to an agent, the agent often returns `no_changes` with reasoning like "this is an epic, needs child-bead breakdown, not monolithic execution" — wasting a worker attempt, leaving the bead in a half-owned claimed state, and forcing manual operator intervention.

Concrete recent example: `agent-6c7a4c11` (Harness × Model Matrix Benchmark — initial tranche) on this user's `agent` repo was dispatched as a single bead, the agent declined to execute monolithically, the worker exited without unclaiming. The bead is now stuck claimed by a dead worker with no change applied.

The fix is to run a triage step **before** Claim: evaluate whether the bead is atomic (one PR's worth of work, single AC focus) or decomposable (multiple distinct deliverables, AC items map to separable PRs). If decomposable, run a splitter that produces child beads (parent-linked, AC-distributed), and skip dispatch of the parent. Children themselves re-enter triage — recursive decomposition with a depth cap.

## Concrete change

Insert a `complexityGate` phase in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` between bead-pick and Claim:

```
next-candidate → ResolveRoute → preflight gate (ddx-98e6e9ef)
  → complexityGate (NEW)
  → if atomic:        Claim → invoke
  → if decomposable:  splitter → file children with parent link → mark parent blocked-by-children → re-pick
  → if ambiguous:     surface to human triage (event + label), don't claim
```

Two prompts, both shipped with the default DDx plugin under `library/prompts/triage/`:

1. **`complexity-eval.md`** — given bead body + AC + deps + history (prior attempts, reviewer BLOCKs), classify `{atomic, decomposable, ambiguous}` with reasoning + confidence.
2. **`bead-split.md`** — given a decomposable bead, emit N child beads (JSON) with: title, AC, parent link, spec-id inheritance, in-scope files subset, labels. Children must collectively cover the parent's AC.

Both prompts use the embedded `agent` harness with profile=default (cheap tier, local-first) — the gate's cost/latency must be << a wasted dispatch.

## Historical evidence engineering

Both prompts must be engineered against historical evidence, not vibes. Harvest a corpus from `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (this project + the user's `agent` project, which has 5 months of bead history including known epic-rejection cases):

- Beads closed `no_changes` whose status text mentions "epic", "split", "breakdown", "scope", or "monolithic" → positive examples for **decomposable**.
- Beads with `attempt_count >= 3` or repeated `kind:reopen` events from reviewer BLOCKs citing scope → positive examples for **decomposable**.
- Beads that were eventually split manually (parent epic + N child tasks filed in a single tracker batch) → use parent body as splitter input, the actual children as ground-truth output.
- Beads closed cleanly on first attempt with single-file or small-diff commits → positive examples for **atomic**.

Hold out 20% as eval. Report `complexity-eval` accuracy/F1 on classification; report `bead-split` AC-coverage rate (children's AC tokens together cover parent's AC) and child-count distribution vs. ground-truth.

The harvest script (`scripts/triage/harvest-corpus.go` or equivalent) is committed and reproducible.

## Recursion + depth cap

Children re-enter the gate. Default depth cap = 3, configurable via `agent.triage.max_decomposition_depth` in `.ddx/config.yaml`. At cap: surface to human (`kind:triage-overflow` event, `status=blocked`, `label=needs-human-decomposition`). Never silently dispatch a parent that hit cap.

## In-scope files

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` — new `complexityGate` phase pre-Claim
- `cli/internal/agent/triage.go` (new) — gate logic, prompt invocation, child filing
- `library/prompts/triage/complexity-eval.md` (new)
- `library/prompts/triage/bead-split.md` (new)
- `cli/internal/agent/triage_test.go` (new) — unit tests against held-out historical corpus
- `library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl` (new) — frozen eval slice (training slice can be regenerated)
- `scripts/triage/harvest-corpus.go` (new) — corpus regeneration
- `docs/triage/decomposition.md` (new) — operator docs

## Out of scope (file follow-ups if/when needed)

- Backfill triage on already-claimed in-flight beads (e.g. unsticking agent-6c7a4c11 retroactively) — operational bead, not part of this feature.
- A web UI for visualizing the triage decision per bead — consumer bead.
- Per-team customization of split rules — premature; ship the default and iterate.

## Anti-goals

- Do **not** rewrite an existing bead in place. Splitting always files children; the parent stays as an epic with `status=blocked-by-children`.
- Do **not** make the gate silently optional. If disabled via flag or config, emit a one-time warning per worker boot.
- Do **not** use a model larger than the cheap tier for the gate prompts.
- Do **not** duplicate routing logic in the gate; it should be a thin caller of the agent SDK with the gate's two prompts.

## Why P1

The failure mode it prevents — claimed-but-undeliverable beads — corrupts the queue's claim semantics, wastes operator time, and silently inflates `attempt_count` on epics that will never close. Without this gate, the rest of the routing-cleanup work (`ddx-fdd3ea36` umbrella) ships into a queue with structurally bad inputs.","events_attachment":"ddx-5bf4ee7e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-5bf4ee7e","issue_type":"epic","labels":["area:dispatch","area:bead-triage","kind:feat","phase:design"],"notes":"Decomposed during queue drain into child beads ddx-26058d78 (corpus harvester), ddx-212c5a00 (prompt assets), ddx-b70c71aa (pre-claim gate wiring), and ddx-ae15ab62 (operator docs).
