- [ ] The doc is reviewed by someone who did NOT write it before landing
- [ ] A new operator can follow the runbook to add a secondary machine without assistance
- [ ] Existing patterns (checkpoint commits, ort merges) are documented as legacy and expected to disappear post-P0-beads","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:36.832469975Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-b0374901","depends_on_id":"ddx-bd42f640","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-b0374901","depends_on_id":"ddx-83ec1430","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-b0374901","depends_on_id":"ddx-6aa50e57","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"}],"description":"Sixth and final child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. The axon divergence happened partly because the multi-machine topology was never written down. Two machines (eitri and sindri) both ran execute-loop against the same project, neither was designated authoritative, and nobody had defined the pull/push contract. The result was predictable once you look at it, but there was no document that said 'this is wrong' before it happened.

## Required documentation

Write docs/EXECUTE-LOOP.md (or expand the ddx README) covering:

### Topology models

**Single-machine solo**: one machine, one execute-loop. No coordination needed beyond the fetch/push/rebase triad (ddx-bd42f640, ddx-83ec1430, ddx-6aa50e57).

**Primary + secondary(s)**: one machine is authoritative (pushes to origin). Secondaries pull-only — they can run the execute-loop if and only if the fetch bead guarantees they fast-forward from origin before every bead, and they refuse to land any work locally. Useful for running review/eval passes in parallel without forking.

**Peer mesh**: multiple machines all push to origin. Each must implement the fetch → verify ancestry → rebase → push invariant from the P0 beads. Requires the tracker concurrency bead (ddx-2791dc4f) to be solved or all machines will step on each other's beads.jsonl.

### Invariants

- Before claiming a bead: local HEAD must be a strict ancestor of origin/<default-branch> (or equal). If not, refuse to claim.
- After landing a bead successfully: push --ff-only or mark land_conflict and stop. Never leave work trapped locally.
- Between sibling workers on the same machine: rebase onto the latest master, never merge.
- Every bead claim must be visible to every other execute-loop before the claimer starts work (tracker concurrency).

### Operator runbook

- How to add a new worker machine safely (fresh clone + verify divergence is 0 + start loop)
- How to recover from a diverged state (the current axon situation): reconcile manually, reset secondaries to origin, restart loops
- How to migrate existing diverged projects to the new safety model (one-time cleanup)
- How to stop all execute-loops globally (e.g., during a freeze window)

### Troubleshooting

- 'divergence_detected' event: what caused it and how to recover
- 'push_rejected' event: what caused it and how to recover
- 'checkpoint before merge' commits in history: legacy pattern, should not appear after ddx-6aa50e57 lands
- Multiple 'Merge made by ort' commits: same as above, legacy pattern

### ADR reference

Once the tracker concurrency bead (ddx-2791dc4f) picks option A / B / C, cite the ADR here so operators understand the coordination model.

## Why this matters

The P0 beads will prevent future divergence, but operators need to understand WHY the rules exist to avoid bypassing them with --force or manual git operations. Documentation is what keeps the fixes effective a year from now.","id":"ddx-b0374901","issue_type":"task","labels":["docs","execute-loop","orchestration"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-ee39eb4","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: document multi-machine topology and operator contract","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:45:17.048232166Z"}

2. `ddx work` does not emit `routing under-specified` for any bead unless the global agent config is itself empty — that case must produce a clear error pointing at the global config, not a per-project routing flag list.

3. Triage complexity gate is enabled by default. The warning 'triage complexity gate is disabled; coarse beads may waste dispatch attempts' must not appear in the default invocation. Opt-out flag exists and is documented.

4. Auto-tier selection picks a tier from bead metadata alone (labels, kind, estimated scope) for beads that have those fields, with the global default_provider for routing when no per-project config is present.

5. Coarse-bead handling: when no tier can confidently execute a bead (e.g. it spans more than N files or N hours of estimated work), DDx either decomposes the bead (records this as decomposition events on the bead) or emits a `needs_triage` status (not `execution_failed`) with a clear reason.

6. Failure recovery: transient failures (network, rate limit, timeout) retry within the selected tier; substantive failures (probe_ok+exec_fail or harness errors) escalate to the next tier per existing escalation logic.

7. Integration test in the DDx test suite: `tests/integration/zero-config-work_test.go` (or equivalent) creates a temp project with one bead and a minimal global config, runs `ddx work --once --local`, asserts the bead closes successfully with no project config files created.

Documentation update: `docs/getting-started.md` (or equivalent) shows the zero-config flow as the default, with project config presented as an advanced override rather than a prerequisite.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"e2f720e85b198785adf09c7e699b7921683bea1b","created_at":"2026-05-01T20:13:29.172609315Z","description":"Operators should not need to author any project-level routing config (.ddx/config.yaml, --harness, --profile, --model, --provider) for `ddx work` to drain a project's bead queue. The platform should auto-select harness, model, and provider based on bead complexity, retry/fall back on failures, and decompose work that is too coarse for one dispatch.

## Observed behavior (HELIX project, 2026-05-01)

Running `ddx work --once --local` from `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`:

```
warning: triage complexity gate is disabled; coarse beads may waste dispatch attempts
▶ helix-13cfe23f: Align HELIX execution contract to shipped DDx execute-loop / execute-bead capabilities
✓ helix-13cfe23f → error: routing under-specified: pass at least one of Harness/Profile/ModelRef/Model/Provider, or auto-selection inputs
project: /Users/erik/Projects/helix
completed: 1  |  successes: 0  |  failures: 1
```

Workarounds tried, all failed or were rejected by users as the wrong layer to address this:

1. `ddx work --once --local --profile cheap` — same routing-under-specified error.
2. `ddx work --once --local --harness openrouter` — `harness "openrouter": no configured provider matches type "openrouter" (available: [])`, despite `~/.config/agent/config.yaml` having multiple openrouter-typed providers.
3. `--min-tier cheap` — unknown flag.

By contrast, the same `ddx work` invocation succeeds for the DDx project itself — workers in `ddx server workers list` show recent successful runs against ddx-* beads. So the per-project config gap is the difference, not anything global.

## Expected behavior

`ddx work` (and `ddx agent execute-loop` directly) should:

1. Auto-select routing for each bead with no flags and no project config required, given that the global agent config (`~/.config/agent/config.yaml`) has providers configured.
2. Make routing decisions from bead complexity: doc-only or single-file changes → cheap tier; multi-file refactors → standard; cross-cutting work → smart. The 'triage complexity gate' warning suggests this exists but is disabled by default.
3. Retry on transient failure within the same tier; escalate to a higher tier on substantive failure (this is documented behavior — `winning_tier=exhausted` events show the escalation chain works once dispatched, but auto-routing has to dispatch first).
4. When a bead is too coarse for any tier to execute reliably, either decompose it into child beads or surface a 'needs operator triage' status with a clear reason — never silently wedge with `routing under-specified`.
5. Default to using the global agent config's `default_provider` when no project override exists, instead of treating the absence of project config as a hard error.

## Why this matters

- **Adoption friction**: Every new project that adopts DDx hits this wall. Authoring `.ddx/config.yaml` requires understanding DDx's routing internals before an operator can run `ddx work` once.
- **Methodology coupling**: HELIX's value proposition includes 'supervised autopilot' — the autopilot is supposed to drain ready beads. When `ddx work` requires per-project routing config, HELIX projects have to ship operational setup that distracts from the methodology.
- **Operator surprise**: The DDx-vs-HELIX boundary contract (HELIX/CONTRACT-001) explicitly says HELIX should not own routing config. But projects can't run `ddx work` without it. That's a contract violation manifesting as setup burden.
- **Triage gate disabled by default**: The warning 'triage complexity gate is disabled; coarse beads may waste dispatch attempts' is emitted on every run but the operator has no obvious way to enable it. The default should be enabled with a documented opt-out, not the inverse.

## Suggested approach (not prescriptive)

1. Audit the auto-selection input requirements: what inputs are needed to pick a tier when no project config is present? Likely the bead's labels, `kind`, file-touch estimate, and prior-attempt history. All available without operator input.
2. Make 'use global agent config defaults' the fallback when project routing is unspecified — currently the code requires explicit project routing OR explicit flags.
3. Enable triage complexity gate by default; document the opt-out flag.
4. Add an integration test: `ddx work --once --local` succeeds against a fresh project with no `.ddx/config.yaml` and a single bead in `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, given a minimal `~/.config/agent/config.yaml` with one provider.

## Out of scope

- Changing the global agent config schema. The fix is to reduce dependency on per-project config, not redesign the global one.
- Per-bead model overrides for advanced users — those should remain available via flags but should not be required for the common case.

## Reference

- HELIX alignment review where this surfaced: `docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-05-01-public-examples.md` (in the easel/helix repo). The audit's 12 follow-up beads were drained via direct sub-agent dispatch instead of `ddx work` because of this gap.","events_attachment":"ddx-b790449b/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":2,"id":"ddx-b790449b","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","area:routing","area:cli","kind:enhancement","priority:high"],"priority":1,"session_id":"eb-1d66753f","status":"closed","title":"ddx work should auto-route execution without project config","updated_at":"2026-05-02T01:42:42.099379643Z"}

Execute-loop treats master as a private branch and produces divergent histories that never reconcile with origin.

## Evidence

On axon, two machines running execute-loop (`eitri` and `sindri`) produced 14 days of independent history from a shared merge-base at 27c54ded (2026-04-06):

- eitri: 134 local commits that never reached origin
- sindri: 336 commits that pushed to origin
- Same FEAT-026 slot used for two unrelated features (sync protocol on eitri, markdown templates on sindri)
- Same beads.jsonl touched by both machines with divergent bead IDs and lifecycle state
- `refs/ddx/iterations/` preservation never fired because merges kept succeeding textually while producing semantically incoherent state
- 12+ "Merge made by 'ort' strategy" commits on 2026-04-13 afternoon on eitri alone, each bringing in a sibling local execute-bead worker's output

## Root causes identified

1. No `git fetch origin` at the top of a worker loop iteration — each bead starts from the worker's stale local HEAD (child bead DDX-agent-fetch)
2. No `git push` on successful landing — successful work stays trapped on the local branch (child bead DDX-agent-push)
3. Intra-machine sibling workers land via `git merge --no-edit` (ort strategy) instead of rebase, producing a fan of merge commits instead of a linear history (child bead DDX-agent-rebase)
4. .ddx/beads.jsonl is a git-tracked mutable ledger with no concurrent-write semantics (child bead DDX-agent-tracker)
5. No divergence monitoring — the 134/336 split grew silently over two weeks (child bead DDX-agent-monitor)
6. Multi-machine topology undocumented — which machine is authoritative, who pulls, who pushes (child bead DDX-agent-topology)

## Primary source evidence

- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:59-69 — Merge function uses `git merge --no-edit` with 'ort' strategy, no rebase alternative, commits a 'chore: checkpoint before merge' before attempting each merge
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_commit.go — has ZERO references to push/fetch/origin
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:~320 — worker loop runs Executor.Execute without any fetch-origin or ancestry-check step

## Impact

Any project that has run the execute-loop for more than a few days across more than one machine is silently divergent from origin. Content written by this session cannot be pushed to the axon origin without a multi-hour manual reconciliation. The axon repo currently has two weeks of unshared work on two machines.

## Scope of this epic

Six child beads covering: fetch-before-work, push-on-success, rebase-vs-merge, tracker concurrency, divergence monitoring, multi-machine topology docs. Each is actionable and self-contained.

## Blocks

Any further `ddx agent execute-loop` runs on projects with more than one participating machine should be paused until at least the first three child beads (fetch, push, rebase) are landed. Running the loop in the current state compounds divergence.","id":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","issue_type":"epic","labels":["bug","execute-loop","orchestration","epic"],"notes":"## Reframed 2026-04-14 — design anchor: ddx-8746d8a6

The six original child beads (bd42f640, 6aa50e57, 83ec1430, 2791dc4f, 42dd982c, b0374901) identified pieces of the gap correctly but did not compose into a coherent end-to-end design. Per an opus-led architectural review on 2026-04-14, the root problem is not that execute-loop's git operations are missing pieces; it is that the whole model is wrong.

Target model (from the ddx user): human-PR queue. Workers are contributors, a centralized land coordinator is the maintainer, target-ref writes are serialized through a single writer that always sees the current tip.

The design for this model is in **ddx-8746d8a6 "execute-loop: adopt human-PR landing model end-to-end (meta redesign)"**. Treat ddx-8746d8a6 as the authoritative design anchor for this epic. The per-bug children below continue to describe individual gap points, but their FIX is the coordinator pattern, not piecemeal patches:

- ddx-bd42f640 (fetch+ancestry before claim) → subsumed by the coordinator's fetch step
- ddx-6aa50e57 (rebase instead of merge) → implemented by the coordinator's rebase step (add land-lock note to acceptance)
- ddx-83ec1430 (push on landing) → part of the coordinator's sequence
- ddx-42dd982c (divergence monitoring) → reframed as coordinator rejection/conflict metrics
- ddx-b0374901 (multi-machine docs) → reframed as per-machine coordinator + push-to-origin
- ddx-2791dc4f (tracker concurrency) → STAYS SEPARATE — tracker is a second shared resource with its own lock story

New P0 child filed alongside: **ddx-e14efc58 "server runWorker never calls LandBeadResult; commits silently lost"** — acute correctness bug discovered during the review. The server path today drops worker commits entirely. Interim fix mirrors the --local closure's land sequence; long-term fix is absorbed by ddx-8746d8a6.

Rollout: implement ddx-8746d8a6 first (the coordinator), then close the subsumed children as their work is done, then close this epic. Do NOT try to land the individual children as standalone patches — the piecemeal approach is what got us here.

Do NOT run execute-loop against the ddx repo (or any other repo) under this epic's pattern until the coordinator is in place. The dirty-tracker problem is constant because workers churn beads.jsonl every few minutes during execute-loop drains. Manual sync is friction; missing a sync risks divergent tracker state.

There is no good remote-only solution: routines in Anthropic cloud cannot touch a local working tree, and a server-side sync only helps if every endpoint is the server (DDx is per-project local).

REQUESTED FEATURE

1. ddx sync — one-shot command that runs the canonical flow:
   a. git fetch origin
   b. if working tree is dirty in tracked DDx-managed paths only (.ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/executions/, .ddx/plugins/), git stash
   c. git pull --rebase=false origin main (merge, never rebase — preserves execute-bead history per the project's standing merge policy)
   d. git stash pop
   e. stage and commit DDx-managed dirty paths (.ddx/beads.jsonl as 'chore: tracker', .ddx/executions/ as 'chore: add execution evidence')
   f. git push origin main; on non-fast-forward, retry from (a) once
   - Refuses to touch any path outside the DDx-managed allowlist.
   - Never uses --force, --no-verify, or destructive flags.
   - Aborts cleanly with a structured exit status if stash-pop conflicts or push fails twice.
   - Exit 0 = clean; non-zero = something needs human attention.

2. ddx sync --watch (or 'ddx sync daemon') — runs the above on an interval (default 15min, configurable). Local-only, runs in foreground or as a backgrounded process.

3. ddx-server can run sync as part of its loop when a project is registered with the server. The server already runs continuously per-project; adding sync to its loop costs almost nothing and turns the server into the canonical multi-machine sync hub.

4. Conflict surface: when sync aborts, doctor should pick up the issue ('ddx doctor' surfaces 'sync aborted at <timestamp>: <reason>') so users see it without having to remember to check.

5. Cross-platform: must work on macOS and Linux at minimum. No symlinks, no bash-isms. Probably implemented in Go using existing git-shell-out helpers (cli/internal/git/).

OUT OF SCOPE
- Conflict resolution beyond stash-pop (that's the preserved-iteration problem; ddx-0097af14 covers).
- Sync of paths outside the DDx-managed allowlist (intentional — never touches user code).
- Multi-remote sync (single origin only for v1).

ACCEPTANCE OF FAILURE: when something can't be auto-resolved (stash-pop conflict, double push fail, divergent base after fetch+merge), 'ddx sync' exits with a structured non-zero code and doctor surfaces it. No retries, no force, no surprise commits.

EVIDENCE THIS IS NEEDED: in the 2026-04-29 dogfood session, the operator manually invoked the sync flow ~6 times in ~3 hours while workers chewed through beads. FEAT-006 Profile Routing section updated with the ladder semantics and the intent table (default/cheap/fast/smart).","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"93f938c41c93be78fc199bd150f455add6575d24","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:51:49.512908486Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-bbb65768","depends_on_id":"ddx-2d974641","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:52:01Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-bbb65768","depends_on_id":"ddx-e7254128","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T13:58:09Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-bbb65768","depends_on_id":"ddx-7bc0c8d5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:36:21Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-bbb65768","depends_on_id":"ddx-ea7f041c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:36:16Z"}],"description":"## Target UX

The user-facing profile vocabulary (cheap / default / fast / smart) should map to concrete routing policies that take full advantage of local models without blocking on their limits. Specifically:

### default profile (the common case)
Try local models first, escalate to cloud if:
- local model's context window is too small for the bead prompt
- local model lacks a capability the bead requires (tools, structured output, etc.)
- local dispatch fails (provider unreachable, timeout, model-side error)
- bead reopens due to post-run check failure (review-malfunction, land-conflict, etc.)

Escalation target: next tier up in the ladder (standard cloud — e.g. codex gpt-5.4 within subscription). If THAT fails, escalate once more to smart cloud. Then stop and unclaim.

### cheap profile
Local only. Never escalate. Intended for high-volume exploratory work where cost dominates and adequate-quality is sufficient. If the cheap tier can't serve the bead, the bead unclaims and waits.

### fast profile
Cloud-first, skip local entirely. Intended for latency-critical or interactive work where local inference's 9s warmup is unacceptable. Starts at a fast cloud model (e.g. gemini-flash or haiku) and escalates to smart on failure.

### smart profile
Cloud-high-quality from the start. Intended for bead-review, adversarial checks, or beads whose AC demands high-quality reasoning. No escalation — if smart fails, the work fails meaningfully.

## Why this matters (dogfooding signal)

As of 2026-04-18, DDx's default is cheap = qwen/qwen3.6 local (vidar-omlx). With v0.3.14 shipping the SSE-comment-frame filter, the local path actually works end-to-end. The queue can drain mostly on local hardware for pennies per bead, escalating only when the bead genuinely needs cloud quality. That's the throughput-per-dollar goal (goal:throughput-per-dollar label).

## Prereqs (already filed under ddx-2d974641 or sibling beads)

This epic is blocked on the autorouting epic delivering its exit-gate UX — 'declare provider + use canonical ref + autorouting works'. Specifically:

- ddx-8610020e (RouteRequest.Provider field) — foundation
- ddx-0486e601 (fuzzy normalization + per-provider) — lets cheap-tier model refs resolve to provider-native IDs
- ddx-2f5a2284 (catalog/Profile discovery integration) — lets Profile tier overrides retarget to provider-native IDs
- ddx-3c5ba7cc (tier escalation --provider affinity) — the lower half of this epic's 'escalate on failure' semantics
- ddx-4817edfd (capability gating) — prevents routing to a model that can't serve the bead

With those in place, the profile-level policy here is mostly CONFIGURATION + a small amount of scoring-layer logic.

## Scope

### Config schema additions

.ddx/config.yaml agent.routing extends with an explicit per-profile ladder:

    agent:
      routing:
        profile_ladders:
          default: [cheap, standard, smart]      # local first, escalate
          cheap:   [cheap]                        # local only
          fast:    [fast, smart]                  # cloud fast, escalate to smart
          smart:   [smart]                        # cloud high-quality, no escalation
        model_overrides:
          cheap:    qwen/qwen3.6
          fast:     kimi/k2.5
          standard: codex/gpt-5.4                # new: subscription cloud, mid-quality
          smart:    minimax/minimax-m2.7

Current profile_priority field (a flat list) becomes a legacy alias for profile_ladders.default with a deprecation warning.

### Tier-escalation policy (routing.go)

For a given profile P, iterate profile_ladders[P]:
- Evaluate model_overrides[tier] resolves on a viable candidate (with --provider affinity from ddx-3c5ba7cc).
- If yes, try it; on failure (execution_failed / land_conflict / post_run_check_failed), move to next tier.
- Record tier decision + escalation chain in routing event on the bead so telemetry reflects where work actually landed.

### User-facing CLI

- ddx work --profile default — the new primary invocation. Most users run this.
- ddx work --profile cheap / fast / smart — opt-in deviations.
- --max-tier continues to cap the ladder (explicit user override beats config).

### Telemetry + measurement

Every execution event records: requested_profile, requested_tier, resolved_tier, escalation_count, final_tier. Aggregated metrics (FEAT-016 / ddx-4b59b816) report per-profile throughput, per-tier cost, per-bead escalation rate.

## Exit gate

'ddx work --profile default --once --local' against a ready bead on a host with a healthy local provider AND healthy cloud fallback:
- Claims the bead.
- Tries cheap tier (local). If it succeeds, closes bead with tier=cheap recorded.
- If cheap fails for any escalation-triggering reason, automatically retries at standard tier without operator intervention.
- If standard fails, retries at smart.
- Final outcome recorded faithfully (success at whatever tier landed, or exhaustion if all tiers failed).

## Out of scope

- Dynamic profile selection ('auto-pick profile based on bead complexity'). Too much magic; explicit profile choice is fine.
- Cost budgets / circuit breakers. FEAT-014 (token awareness) tracks this separately.
- Per-bead profile overrides (bead-level 'requires-profile: smart'). Could be a future follow-up but out of scope here.

## Metadata

Governing spec: FEAT-006 (agent service) + the autorouting epic ddx-2d974641. This bead is the PROFILE-layer policy that sits on top of the routing fixes from ddx-2d974641. Do not start until ddx-2d974641's Phase-1 children are closed.","events_attachment":"ddx-bbb65768/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-bbb65768","issue_type":"epic","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:design","area:agent","area:routing","area:profiles","goal:throughput-per-dollar","wave:deferred","workstream:agent-upgrade"],"priority":2,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"profile/automation: default = local-first with escalation; fast/smart = cloud-first","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:03:22.145529072Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:763:{"acceptance":"Regression test in cli/internal/server/workers_test.go: starts a WorkerManager against a temp project (real git repo fixture), seeds one open bead, runs a worker whose agent-run produces a single commit, waits for runWorker to exit, asserts (a) git log on the project's target branch shows the worker's commit SHA, (b) the WorkerRecord.LastResult.ResultRev matches the SHA visible in git log, (c) ddx bead show returns status=closed with the commit SHA recorded as the closing session. Test must fail pre-fix and pass post-fix. The workers.go:runWorker closure at line 248 calls LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult before returning from the executor. `ddx agent execute-loop --harness claude --model sonnet` (no --local) run against the ddx repo with one ready bead advances main by at least one commit, visible via git log main -1.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T14:36:47.352994047Z","description":"Server-managed execute-loop workers produce commits that never reach the target branch. This is the default path when an operator runs `ddx agent execute-loop` without `--local`, yet the commits are silently lost.

## Evidence

cli/internal/server/workers.go:runWorker (function at line ~237) builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc closure at line 248:

    executor := agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
        runner := m.buildAgentRunner(m.projectRoot)
        gitOps := &agent.RealGitOps{}

        res, err := agent.ExecuteBead(m.projectRoot, beadID, agent.ExecuteBeadOptions{
            Harness: spec.Harness,
            Model:   spec.Model,
            Effort:  spec.Effort,
        }, gitOps, runner)
        if err != nil {
            return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, err
        }
        return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{...}, nil
    })

This closure calls agent.ExecuteBead directly and returns the ExecuteBeadReport with ResultRev populated — but never calls agent.LandBeadResult. The commit sits in the worktree's branch; nothing advances the project's target branch.

Grep confirms LandBeadResult has exactly two production call sites in the entire repo:

    cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1372   — inside runAgentExecuteLoop's --local branch
    cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:134  — interactive `ddx agent execute-bead` CLI

Neither is reachable from cli/internal/server/workers.go.

## Why this matters

Under the current help text, `ddx agent execute-loop --harness claude --model sonnet` (no --local) is the default and submits to the server. Per the help:

    --local    Run inline in current process instead of server worker
               (default: submit to server)

Every operator who follows the documented default pattern is running workers whose commits will not land. The loop reports ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess to the tracker (CloseWithEvidence fires), closes the bead, and writes tracker events — but `git log main` never sees the commit.

## Scope of this bead

This bead is a P0 CALL-SITE FIX. It does NOT redesign the merge orchestration — that is covered by ddx-8746d8a6 (the meta-bead). This bead closes the acute correctness gap: ensure the server path, today, lands commits the same way the --local path does, so work stops being silently dropped while the meta redesign is in flight.

## The fix (interim, pre-meta)

Mirror the --local closure's land sequence into workers.go:248. After agent.ExecuteBead returns a non-nil result, call agent.LandBeadResult + agent.ApplyLandingToResult before returning the ExecuteBeadReport. Pseudocode:

    res, err := agent.ExecuteBead(...)
    if err != nil && res == nil {
        return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, err
    }
    if res != nil {
        if landing, landErr := agent.LandBeadResult(
            m.projectRoot, res, &agent.RealOrchestratorGitOps{}, agent.BeadLandingOptions{},
        ); landErr == nil {
            agent.ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
        } else if err == nil {
            err = landErr
        }
    }
    if err != nil {
        return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, err
    }
    return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{...populated from res...}, nil

This IS the same merge-instead-of-rebase pattern the meta redesign will replace — but it at least stops losing work today. The meta redesign (ddx-8746d8a6) will rewrite this call site entirely, so consider this a stopgap until the coordinator goroutine is in place.

## Regression test

cli/internal/server/workers_test.go: start a WorkerManager against a temp project, submit a worker that produces a single commit, wait for the worker to finish, assert git log on the temp project's target branch shows the worker's commit is in the history and referenced by the worker's LastResult.ResultRev. The test must fail against the current code (commit is NOT in main's history post-worker-exit) and pass after the fix.

## Dependency

Do NOT merge this fix onto main until the meta-bead (ddx-8746d8a6) design is approved. The stopgap uses the flawed `git merge --no-edit` Merge() path and will produce 2-parent merge commits. The alternative is to wait for the meta redesign's Land() coordinator and skip the interim. The tradeoff:

- Land interim fix now: stops silent work loss, accepts ongoing merge-commit noise until the meta redesign.
- Skip interim fix: silent work loss continues (every server-worker bead's commits never reach main), but the main branch history stays cleaner.

Recommendation: DO land the interim fix. Silent work loss is worse than merge-commit noise because it destroys the worker's output. Merge-commit noise is cosmetic and gets flattened by the meta redesign anyway.","id":"ddx-e14efc58","issue_type":"bug","parent":"ddx-8746d8a6","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-43009cc","status":"closed","title":"P0 bug: server runWorker never calls LandBeadResult; commits silently lost","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:26:48.485315837Z"}

Define the progress-event contract for managed embedded-agent runs so DDx can surface meaningful live execution status in the CLI and server/UI.

## Goals
- Define structured phase/heartbeat output for embedded-agent runs (queueing, launch, running, post-checks, landing, preserved, done)
- Define the minimal per-event fields needed for operator visibility and later reporting: worker/project/bead/run identity, phase, elapsed/heartbeat timing, harness/provider/model/profile, and token/cost deltas when available
- Clarify which live progress artifacts remain runtime-only and which summaries/indexes are exposed through DDx-owned APIs and UI surfaces
- Keep provider-native transcript ownership external; DDx progress output should be normalized runtime telemetry, not transcript duplication

## Required spec work
- Update FEAT-006, FEAT-002, and FEAT-008 so embedded-agent progress visibility is an explicit contract rather than an implementation detail
- Clarify how progress events relate to tracked execution artifacts, session logs, and server-managed worker state

## Required implementation planning
- Define the server/API/UI read model for live worker progress and recent phase history
- Define how CLI and UI consume the same structured progress feed without introducing hidden workflow policy","events_attachment":"ddx-f5337613/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T05:15:51.523596966Z","id":"ddx-f5337613","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:planning","kind:architecture","area:agent","area:api","area:ui","area:docs"],"owner":"ddx","parent":"ddx-8b6cd40e","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-82ff773","spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Add structured embedded-agent progress output for workers and UI","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:12:55.000438268Z"}

(3) cli/internal/server/workers_test.go TestWorkerManagerStartAndShow (or a sibling test) exercises the server-worker path and asserts the same clean-worktree guarantee. (6) No changes to AGENTS.md, FEAT-006, or skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md — the policy is already correct; this is implementation only.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"ce25dda3c76977d7d66e0a39dc6e93b0f2b25183","created_at":"2026-04-15T15:34:35.535203989Z","description":"## Scope

execute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it
materializes (`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,
`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run
returns. Successful bead commits reach main via the "Merge bead" path, but the
evidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that
gap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and
returns the working tree clean.

Concrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill
that imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/` directories
(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.
That backfill must not need to happen again.

## Files to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the
  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and
  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and
  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:
  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's
      evidence files, single commit), OR
  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [<attempt-id>]` commit
      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same
      merge transaction.
  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent
  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must
  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent
  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded
  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After
  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,
  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the
  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/`),
  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise ("orchestrator left worktree
  dirty: <path>").
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,
  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and
  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean
  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping
  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.

## Files NOT to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead
  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /
  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). Those are agent-facing and must keep
  telling the agent to `git add <specific-paths>` only. The fix belongs in
  the orchestrator, not the prompt: DDx wrote the evidence, DDx commits it.
- `AGENTS.md`, `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md`,
  `skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md` — the policy is already correct as of commit
  3a2a0c5. This bead implements the policy; it does not amend it.

## Cross-Bead Context

- Parent epic `ddx-32b3008e` ("Evolve execute-bead into a tracked
  execution-evidence workflow", P0, spec-id FEAT-006) is the meta for this
  work. This bead is the first execution-layer task under that epic.
- The trail-preservation policy is codified in commit 3a2a0c5: AGENTS.md
  "Merge Policy" section, FEAT-006 "Execution Trail Preservation in
  Downstream Merges" section (with 3 acceptance criteria), and
  skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md "Branch Merge Policy" section.
- The land flow today uses a mix of `git merge --no-ff` and
  `git update-ref` (for bare-repo worktrees) — see
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:166-544 for the three code paths
  that touch the merge. Any of them may be the right insertion point; pick
  the one that runs LAST after the agent commit is finalized.
- The bug-fix commit 2792dfc ("anchor SessionLogDir to project root") gives
  the orchestrator a reliable absolute path for session logs. This bead is
  the analogous fix for execution evidence.

## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)

> **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]
> Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git
> history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports
> read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`
> contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from
> git history alone.
>
> **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**
> - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker
>   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then
>   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target
>   branch HEAD after the merge.
> - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing
>   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e
>   <closing_commit_sha>` still succeeds on main after the merge.

This bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the
per-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely
preserved across merges.

## Verification Commands

- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestExecuteBeadLandsEvidence`
  passes (new regression test, see AC below).
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd` passes
  (full suite; no regressions).
- Manual dogfooding: in a throwaway clone, run
  `ddx agent execute-loop --once` against a ready bead. After it returns,
  `git status --porcelain` MUST print nothing. `git log -1 --stat` MUST show
  at least one file under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` in the HEAD commit
  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).","events_attachment":"ddx-f7ac7826/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-f7ac7826","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:exec","area:git"],"parent":"ddx-32b3008e","priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"orchestrator: land execution evidence and leave worktree clean after agent run","updated_at":"2026-04-16T18:34:37.770774687Z"}

(2) Fixture reviewer-stream-approve-misextracted.jsonl (from ddx-004eccbc) drives a regression test: the pre-fix behavior records BLOCK; the fix records APPROVE.
(3) ParseReviewVerdict no longer defaults to BLOCK on an unrecognized verdict line. Unrecognized output returns a parse error; the caller handles that as a review-error (feeds ddx-738edf47's retryable-failure path), not a silent BLOCK.

This bead (scope:partial) stops silent-BLOCK mis-records — it does NOT stop mis-extraction itself. After wave:2 lands, the reviewer will still misfire on codex/claude raw stream frames because clause (1) (consume normalized final text) is deferred to ddx-7bc0c8d5. What changes: misfires surface as loud review-error reopens instead of silent BLOCKs, feeding ddx-738edf47's retryable-failure path. Expect a "review-error reopens per closed bead" metric to look rough in this window. The real extraction fix lands when agent-32e8ff5e ships upstream and ddx-7bc0c8d5 migrates.

---

Reporter: nexiq+axon 2026-04-18/19/20. Symptom 1 of review-malfunction report.

The post-merge reviewer in `ddx agent execute-loop` records `{"verdict":"BLOCK"}` in `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/result.json` even when the reviewer LLM produced a well-formed `### Verdict: APPROVE` (or REQUEST_CHANGES) with full AC grade table and cited evidence. Observed ~20 times in a row on nexiq codex+claude loop 2026-04-19 against beads nexiq-b5661a77, nexiq-50485cd3, nexiq-be1bc61d, nexiq-8999acab, hx-8a26c6df, hx-4901d83a, hx-e33210f6, nexiq-35d64a6b, and more — every bead in the run.

Code site: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:76` — `reVerdictLine` regex `^#{1,4}\s+Verdict:\s*(APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK)\s*$`. When the reviewer output contains the raw verdict on a heading line but is wrapped inside a codex streaming JSON event body (e.g. `{"type":"item.completed","item":{..."text":"### Verdict: APPROVE..."}}` with escaped newlines), the regex does not match across the wrapped payload and `ParseReviewVerdict` falls through to the `VerdictBlock` default at line 86 ("safe default: requires human attention"). The bead then gets a `review_malfunction` event with detail "post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)".

Actual reviewer output is preserved in `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` under the reviewer session id and can be recovered post-facto; the parser just fails to see it.

Hypothesis from report: parser anchors on `### Verdict:` heading but a codex harness stream serializes text with `\n` escaping or wraps the reviewer body inside thread/turn JSON events so the multiline anchor never hits. Root fix options: (a) extract reviewer text from the raw stream before parsing (strip codex thread/turn JSON to the inner `item_0.text` payload); (b) switch the reviewer prompt to require machine-readable JSON `{verdict, ac_grades, summary}` and parse that; (c) broaden the regex to match non-anchored `### Verdict: X` headings even inside JSON-escaped text.

## Upstream dependency

Blocked on agent-32e8ff5e (in ~/Projects/agent): the fix moves reviewer-text extraction behind the ddx-agent service boundary so DDx no longer parses raw harness stream frames. Until that lands, f7ae036f's clause (1) on "normalized reviewer text" cannot be satisfied and the regression test from ddx-004eccbc remains red. The parse-error / no-default-BLOCK changes (clauses 3-4) can land independently and are worth doing now.

## Scope split (wave 2)

Clauses (3) and (4) — no default-BLOCK, parse errors surface as review-error, fixture regression test — land in wave 2 with only ddx-004eccbc as a prereq.

Clause (1) — consume normalized final text from the agent service instead of raw stream frames — is deferred to ddx-7bc0c8d5 because it depends on agent-32e8ff5e shipping upstream. This bead is labeled scope:partial to signal that wave 2 only closes (3)+(4); a follow-up pass on (1) happens inside ddx-7bc0c8d5's migration.","id":"ddx-f7ae036f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","kind:review","goal:throughput-per-dollar","wave:2","workstream:reviewer","scope:partial"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: post-merge reviewer mis-extracts verdict, emits spurious BLOCK on APPROVE","updated_at":"2026-04-20T15:50:37.451719605Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:830:{"acceptance":"1. Per-field hard cap of 65,535 bytes on bead.events[].body and adjacent writer paths, documented in cli/internal/bead package comment. bd-compatibility rationale in the comment.
2. Reviewer raw streams never enter bead.events[].body. Reviewer output path writes the full stream to .ddx/executions/<id>/reviewer-stream.log; event body is ≤512 bytes (verdict + first-line rationale + path reference). Unit test: a 2MB reviewer stream produces a <=512-byte event body referencing the full payload on disk.
3. Other writers that append to bead.events[].body enforce the per-field cap; overflow persists as an artifact under .ddx/executions/<id>/ and the event body carries a path reference plus summary.
4. Reader: bead/session scanner buffers raised from the current 1MB to at least 16MB (covers observed extremes: 1.46MB bead row, ~7MB session rows). No new global "shared buffer constant" abstraction — raise each call site's existing Buffer() arg (cli/internal/bead/store.go:172, processmetrics.go:723, session_log_tailer.go:91). claude_stream.go:101 is already 16MB.
5. Regression test: a synthetic beads.jsonl line of 2MB reads cleanly; a line exceeding the buffer surfaces an error naming the bead id and does not crash.
6. bd round-trip smoke: a DDx-authored bead with one 65,535-byte description field exports to JSONL and is accepted by `bd import`. Requires bd on PATH; otherwise t.Skip() with a clear message.

Symptom 2 of review-malfunction report.

A reviewer emitted a ~1.46MB event body (streamed reviewer output dumped verbatim into bead.events[].body). Because cli/internal/bead/store.go:172 sets bufio.Scanner.Buffer to 1MB, subsequent reads of beads.jsonl fail with "bufio.Scanner: token too long." Tracker effectively bricked until the row is hand-trimmed.

## Compatibility constraint (validated 2026-04-20)

Empirical bd test (/tmp/bd-size-test via homebrew bd 1.0.2):
- Per-field insert cap: 65,535 bytes. Exactly Dolt TEXT column limit. 65535 inserts; 65536 errors "string too long."
- Per-line export: easily exceeds 1MB when a bead accumulates multiple long comments/events. Observed: one bead with 5 × 65KB comments → 326KB line. Our 1.46MB corruption is ~23 such events, plausible for a long-running bead.
- bd export lines ARE currently unreadable by DDx when they exceed our 1MB scanner cap — even when no single field is oversized.

## Design contract

- Write-side (DDx): enforce per-field hard cap of 65,535 bytes so DDx-authored beads round-trip through `bd import`. Soft cap ~10KB with head+tail+marker truncation for typical event bodies. Overflow persists as an artifact file under .ddx/executions/<id>/, event body stores path + one-line summary.
- Read-side (DDx): accept arbitrarily long lines from bd-compatible tools. Scanner buffer sized generously (suggest 64MB) so normal accumulated beads never trigger token-too-long.
- Read-side tolerance: if a line does exceed the buffer, surface a clear error with bead id and pointer to the artifact, do not crash.

## Known scanner sites

All currently undersized relative to bd export reality:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:172 (1MB)
- cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:723 (1MB)
- cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:101 (16MB — probably OK)
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_tailer.go:91 (4MB)

## Artifact reference

Test stays in the suite as a regression guard.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:26:11.505315747Z","description":"## Status after codex review (2026-04-22)

Original plan was consolidated in this bead but **reduced in scope** after codex's review surfaced that most of the compatibility and data-model work belongs upstream in ddx-agent. This bead is now the **DDx-side tracker only**. Upstream work is queued as separate beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` (prefix `agent-*`) and will land as a single tagged point release; nothing in DDx lands until that release is pulled.

## Goals (unchanged — user stated 2026-04-22)

**G1. Zero-interaction defaults.** `ddx agent run` and `ddx agent execute-bead` just work with no flags.

**G2. Usable profiles.** `--profile standard`, `--profile local` force clear, documented rules.

**G3. Usable overrides.** `--harness claude` forces claude. `--model opus-4.7` forces opus-4.7. Within requirements: available, quota OK, lowest cost.

## Upstream dependencies (tracked in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/)

This bead blocks on a single upstream point release that carries:

- Profile catalog with first-class `default`, `local`, `standard` (and any others DDx needs to expose) + documented provider-preference rules.
- Supported-models allow-list on routing-inputs `HarnessEntry`; gating rejects non-allowed models even when `ExactPinSupport: true`.
- Typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` surfaced from `Execute` so explicit `--harness X --model Y` conflicts fail fast with actionable detail.
- Public `RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error for "no viable candidate" with per-candidate rejection reasons — DDx's decision-trace consumers need this.
- Cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` (`CostUSDPer1kTokens`) + scoring tiebreak that prefers lower cost, with unknown-cost fallback, subscription-quota pricing, and near-quota demotion.
- Documentation for the profile catalog, override precedence, and "best provider" contract.

Once that release is tagged, DDx work below proceeds.

## DDx-side changes (reduced scope)

**D1. Dispatch sends a real spec.**

`workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker` treats empty input as `{Profile: "default"}` explicitly. Dashboard Drain button calls the same path. No DDx-side default synthesis beyond "profile=default if unset."

**D2. Stop synthesizing a tier ladder by default.**

Default `ddx agent run` / `ddx agent execute-bead` / `ddx work` path calls `agentlib.ResolveRoute(Profile: spec.Profile)` with no Model string unless `spec.Model` was explicitly given. No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.

**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**

After `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.

**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**

Deliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:
- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.
- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.
- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.
- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.

Migration note published in DDx changelog.

**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**

- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")`. No other DDx inputs.
- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.
- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.
- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.

## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)

- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).
- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.

## Sequencing

1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)
2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.
3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.
4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.
5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.

## Cross-references

- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.
- Direct codex review feedback logged in bead notes below.
- Supersedes original 3-step proposal from conversation history.
- Addresses `project_endpoint_routing_design.md` memory directive (2026-04-21): "kill named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match."
- Hard downstream dependents (wait for this bead):
  - `ddx-b6cf025c` — drain-worker count control (default profile must mean something first)
  - `ddx-23978824` — providers UI (needs decision trace from A4)
  - `ddx-6904a90b` — sessions billing-mode (benefits from richer session.routing metadata)
- Independent of this bead: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter).

## Codex review — key corrections folded in

1. A3 confirmed by code inspection: `HarnessEntry` has no model-allow-list field; `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery; `gating.go:88` only checks `ExactPinSupport`. Allow-list must be added to routing inputs/capabilities, not just "fix ExactPin semantics."
2. A4 bigger than originally written: public `RouteDecision` has no `Candidates` field; service drops rejection data on error. Needs typed error + public surface.
3. Earlier claim that codex/claude have `Models: nil` was wrong — that's DDx's local `harness_config.go`, not upstream's `internal/harnesses/registry.go`. Upstream has codex=[gpt-5.4], claude=[sonnet, opus, claude-sonnet-4-6]. Allow-list work is universal upstream.
4. Upstream catalog has no `default` or `local` profile today (models.yaml:195). Must be added before DDx materializes them.
5. D3 restricted to defensive-only; all compatibility logic belongs upstream.
6. G2 precedence conflict (`--profile local --harness claude`): resolved in upstream docs as explicit-pin-wins but upstream returns typed error if the pin violates the profile's hard constraint (e.g., local-only with non-local harness).
7. G3 cost needs explicit data model — no price field on ProviderEntry today.
8. D4 reframed as deliberate breaking change, not warning-only drift.
9. D6 + D7 dropped from epic scope.
10. File: cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:285
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new summary window helper uses bead `updated_at` as the inclusion gate, so a bead whose close fact and only correlated sessions are both before the cutoff still appears in `ddx metrics summary --since ...` if it was edited later for unrelated reasons. `beadsVisibleInSummaryWindow` admits that bead, and the downstream summary logic counts it in `summary.Beads.Total/Closed`, `summary.Beads.UnknownCost`, `summary.Cost.Beads`, and `summary.Cost.Features` even though there are no in-window lifecycle or session facts. Repro: with one bead closed on 2026-01-01, one correlated session on 2026-01-01, and only `updated_at=2026-03-05`, `Summary(Query{Since: 2026-02-01, HasSince: true})` returns `beads.total=1 closed=1 unknown_cost=1 cost.beads=1` while sessions, cycle-time, and rework are all zero. SD-016 says `--since` means include only facts observed on or after the cutoff for the relevant fact type; a tracker metadata edit after the window is not a new lifecycle or cost fact.
Suggested fix: Define summary bead inclusion from the same fact timelines used by the underlying sections, rather than raw `updated_at`. A bead with no in-window close/reopen/session facts should not surface in the windowed summary or its cost/feature counts, and regression coverage should include the late-update/no-facts case.","id":"hx-17a99906","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:cli","area:api"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: summary since window must ignore late-updated pre-cutoff beads","updated_at":"2026-04-10T13:24:32.606755989Z"}

File: cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:208
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 9f96a3e scopes the legacy-session cutoff by harness, but `aggregateUsageAggs` still drops every `sessions.jsonl` row at or after the first routing outcome timestamp for that harness whether or not that session actually has a mirrored routing outcome (`cutoffByHarness` at lines 208-210 and 256-279). That remains an undercount path because session logging and routing-outcome logging are separate best-effort writes: the runner always appends the session first and then ignores any `AppendOutcome` failure (`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:153-163`, `cli/internal/agent/routing_metrics.go:214-256`). If one later routing-outcome append is skipped or fails, `ddx agent usage` silently drops the valid legacy session row for that same harness as soon as any earlier outcome exists.
Suggested fix: Gate legacy-row suppression on actual mirrored identity matches rather than a blanket per-harness timestamp cutoff, or persist an explicit per-row migration marker that proves the session was mirrored before dropping it; add regression coverage for a harness with an earlier routing outcome plus a later legacy-only session row that lacks matching native/trace/span identifiers.","id":"hx-1f1e2472","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:agent","area:cli"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/agent_usage.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: keep same-harness legacy sessions visible when routing outcomes are missing","updated_at":"2026-04-10T13:27:36.885330695Z"}

File: cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:265
Category: correctness

Description: `Summary` accepts a windowed query but still counts all beads and all sessions from `loadInputs()` before applying the cutoff, so `--since` produces internally inconsistent dashboards. In `Summary`, `summary.Beads.Total/Open/Closed` and `summary.Sessions.*` are accumulated from the full stores (`len(beads)` and the unfiltered `sessions` loop), while cycle-time and rework use the cutoff and cost partially windows only per-session math. Description: `sessionCostState` and `sessionDerivedCost` treat any non-positive `cost_usd` as not-known by checking `sess.CostUSD > 0`. File: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md:175
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new minimal-routing schema records `RoutingOutcome`, `QuotaSnapshot`, and `BurnSummary` only by `harness` and `surface`. Earlier in the same design, however, each `CandidatePlan` carries `CanonicalTarget`, `ConcreteModel`, `EstimatedCostUSD`, and `PerformanceMetrics` because routing decisions can differ materially by resolved model or exact pin. A single surface can host very different models with different latency, reliability, and cost characteristics. If DDx aggregates outcome and burn data only at the harness/surface level, runs for one model will contaminate routing signals for another, making model-specific scoring and exact-pin behavior incorrect.
Suggested fix: Extend the minimal routing-metrics schema with a model-identifying dimension such as `canonical_target` or `concrete_model` (or explicitly define an equivalent normalization key), and update FEAT-014/SD-015 so performance, cost, and burn inputs remain attributable to the resolved model rather than only the surface.","id":"hx-4a12a619","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:agent","area:docs"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: keep routing metrics model-specific","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:47:24.607997607Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:975:{"acceptance":"SD-016 states that cost_usd=-1 means unknown rather than known cost, direct cost aggregation excludes the sentinel value, and the cost_state / cost precedence rules align with FEAT-014 and FEAT-006 sentinel semantics","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-09T23:00:02Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2091135","created_at":"2026-04-09T22:35:00.80036448Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-016-process-metrics.md:191
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: SD-016 defines `cost_state=known` whenever a session row reports `cost_usd` and says bead lifecycle cost should use `session.cost_usd` whenever it is present. But FEAT-014 and FEAT-006 explicitly define `cost_usd=-1` as the sentinel for unknown cost, not a real price. Implementing SD-016 as written would treat unknown-cost sessions as known values, producing negative dollar totals and skipping the estimation/unknown fallback for a core FEAT-016 metric.
Suggested fix: Update SD-016 so `cost_usd=-1` is mapped to `cost_state=unknown` and excluded from direct cost sums, with estimation or separate beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` (prefix `agent-*`) and will land as a single tagged point release; nothing in DDx lands until that release is pulled.

## Goals (unchanged — user stated 2026-04-22)

**G1. Zero-interaction defaults.** `ddx agent run` and `ddx agent execute-bead` just work with no flags.

**G2. Usable profiles.** `--profile standard`, `--profile local` force clear, documented rules.

**G3. Usable overrides.** `--harness claude` forces claude. `--model opus-4.7` forces opus-4.7. Within requirements: available, quota OK, lowest cost.

## Upstream dependencies (tracked in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/)

This bead blocks on a single upstream point release that carries:

- Profile catalog with first-class `default`, `local`, `standard` (and any others DDx needs to expose) + documented provider-preference rules.
- Supported-models allow-list on routing-inputs `HarnessEntry`; gating rejects non-allowed models even when `ExactPinSupport: true`.
- Typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` surfaced from `Execute` so explicit `--harness X --model Y` conflicts fail fast with actionable detail.
- Public `RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error for "no viable candidate" with per-candidate rejection reasons — DDx's decision-trace consumers need this.
- Cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` (`CostUSDPer1kTokens`) + scoring tiebreak that prefers lower cost, with unknown-cost fallback, subscription-quota pricing, and near-quota demotion.
- Documentation for the profile catalog, override precedence, and "best provider" contract.

Once that release is tagged, DDx work below proceeds.

## DDx-side changes (reduced scope)

**D1. Dispatch sends a real spec.**

`workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker` treats empty input as `{Profile: "default"}` explicitly. Dashboard Drain button calls the same path. No DDx-side default synthesis beyond "profile=default if unset."

**D2. Stop synthesizing a tier ladder by default.**

Default `ddx agent run` / `ddx agent execute-bead` / `ddx work` path calls `agentlib.ResolveRoute(Profile: spec.Profile)` with no Model string unless `spec.Model` was explicitly given. No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.

**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**

After `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.

**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**

Deliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:
- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.
- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.
- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.
- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.

Migration note published in DDx changelog.

**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**

- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")`. No other DDx inputs.
- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.
- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.
- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.

## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)

- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).
- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.

## Sequencing

1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)
2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.
3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.
4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.
5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.

## Cross-references

- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.
- Direct codex review feedback logged in bead notes below.
- Supersedes original 3-step proposal from conversation history.
- Addresses `project_endpoint_routing_design.md` memory directive (2026-04-21): "kill named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match."
- Hard downstream dependents (wait for this bead):
  - `ddx-b6cf025c` — drain-worker count control (default profile must mean something first)
  - `ddx-23978824` — providers UI (needs decision trace from A4)
  - `ddx-6904a90b` — sessions billing-mode (benefits from richer session.routing metadata)
- Independent of this bead: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter).

## Codex review — key corrections folded in

1. A3 confirmed by code inspection: `HarnessEntry` has no model-allow-list field; `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery; `gating.go:88` only checks `ExactPinSupport`. Allow-list must be added to routing inputs/capabilities, not just "fix ExactPin semantics."
2. A4 bigger than originally written: public `RouteDecision` has no `Candidates` field; service drops rejection data on error. Needs typed error + public surface.
3. Earlier claim that codex/claude have `Models: nil` was wrong — that's DDx's local `harness_config.go`, not upstream's `internal/harnesses/registry.go`. Upstream has codex=[gpt-5.4], claude=[sonnet, opus, claude-sonnet-4-6]. Allow-list work is universal upstream.
4. Upstream catalog has no `default` or `local` profile today (models.yaml:195). Must be added before DDx materializes them.
5. D3 restricted to defensive-only; all compatibility logic belongs upstream.
6. G2 precedence conflict (`--profile local --harness claude`): resolved in upstream docs as explicit-pin-wins but upstream returns typed error if the pin violates the profile's hard constraint (e.g., local-only with non-local harness).
7. G3 cost needs explicit data model — no price field on ProviderEntry today.
8. D4 reframed as deliberate breaking change, not warning-only drift.
9. D6 + D7 dropped from epic scope.
10. Quick-test gates added: upstream exact-pin regression; service-layer typed error preserves reasons; `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` against endpoint-only config; DDx worker exits before any bead claim.

## What is blocking
The bead's notes explicitly warn that B22 closed as already_satisfied without actually retiring the legacy options types. The current tree still uses `agent.RunOptions` extensively:

- Composite-literal usages (RunOptions{...}) across production code:
  cli/internal/agent/{compare_adapter,execute_bead,execute_bead_review,grade,runner,service_run}.go
  cli/internal/exec/store.go
  cli/internal/server/server.go
  cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go
- Function parameters typed as the legacy options across ~15 production files
  (agent_runner.go, agent_runner_service.go, claude_stream.go, compare_adapter.go,
   execute_bead.go, execute_bead_review.go, models.go, routing_metrics.go,
   runner.go, script.go, service_run.go, session_index.go, virtual.go, ...)
- Same pattern repeated across ~20 test files (agent_test.go, claude_stream_test.go,
  models_test.go, prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go, session_index_test.go,
  virtual_test.go, execute_bead_*_test.go, etc.)

Total: 88 violations. This is exactly the state the bead's "REOPENED 2026-04-26" note
predicts: "runtimelint actually run produces ~80 violations against the tree because
B22 didn't actually retire RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions."

## Why this is not a final-sweep task
The "final sweep" bead is intentionally narrow: rerun the lint and assert green to
catch drift introduced after the migration is done. It is not the bead that performs
the migration. Resolving 88 violations across ~25 production files plus ~20 test files
requires:
  - Threading *Runtime + ResolvedConfig through every harness, service, runner,
    compare adapter, execute_bead, claude_stream, virtual, session_index, models,
    routing_metrics, script, exec/store, server, and cmd/agent_cmd call site.
  - Updating every test fixture that constructs a RunOptions{...}.
  - Removing the legacy types themselves once all references are gone.
That is the body of B22, not a sweep. Doing it inside this bead would silently absorb
the entirety of a separate migration bead and mask the fact that B22 is incomplete —
which is precisely the problem the reopened note flags.

## What a follow-up attempt would need
1. B22 (or a successor) must actually land: migrate all RunOptions/CompareOptions/
   QuorumOptions call sites to *Runtime + config.ResolvedConfig and delete the
   legacy types. The runtimelint output above is an exhaustive checklist of the
   remaining call sites.
2. Once B22 is genuinely green (lint exits 0 on the tree), this final-sweep bead
   becomes a one-liner: re-run the same command and confirm no new drift appeared.

Re-queueing this bead now would just produce another no_changes (or, worse, an
attempt that absorbs B22's scope under the wrong bead id). Recommend keeping it
blocked on B22 until B22 ships for real.

## What is blocking

The migration is too large for a single execute-bead worker window even with
the expanded scope. Three concrete reasons:

1. **Per-bead size budget.** SD-024 §Migration discipline (lines 234–256)
   explicitly mandates "<500 LoC of net change, <20 minutes of agent time,
   under $10 of model spend" per bead, with a footnote that "Anything that
   wants to 'do the whole loop migration in one shot' is sized wrong and
   must be split." This bead's expanded scope is ~4,000 production LoC plus
   ~10,000 test LoC — 8x to 28x the SD-mandated budget. The previous attempt
   spent 16.6 minutes and $6.75 producing no_changes; even doubling that
   budget cannot produce a green tree across this surface in one pass.

2. **The migration is not one atomic change but a sequence of seam swaps.**
   Each affected public API (RunViaService → RunWithConfigViaService,
   RunCompareViaService → RunCompareWithConfigViaService, BuildArgs's
   RunOptions parameter, SessionIndexEntryFromResult's RunOptions parameter,
   RunFunc closure type) has its own caller graph. SD-024's own discipline
   (lines 248–256) prescribes per-caller migration beads precisely so each
   step leaves the tree green and lint-clean. Bundling them into one bead
   re-creates the bug class the SD's migration discipline was written to
   avoid: a half-finished refactor with the lint gate firing on the
   intermediate state, where the worker either has to disable hooks (forbidden)
   or revert most of the work to commit anything.

3. **Test-file lint cascade.** The runtime-lint pre-commit gate (lefthook.yml
   pattern 2) flags any staged Go file containing a RunOptions{} / CompareOptions{}
   / QuorumOptions{} composite literal. Migrating BuildArgs's signature
   alone (to take a non-RunOptions parameter) re-stages every one of the
   15+ agent_test.go test files, and the gate fires on each. The same
   cascade fires when SessionIndexEntryFromResult's signature changes
   (re-staging session_index_test.go), when RunFunc's closure type changes
   (re-staging compare/quorum/benchmark tests), and so on for each public-API
   seam swap. Every signature change requires migrating its consumers in
   the *same commit* — but the consumers' test files already contain
   composite literals from sibling test cases that aren't in this bead's
   AC. Those literals stay valid (BuildArgs still works) but the gate fires
   because the file is now staged. Untangling this requires touching every
   composite-literal site in every staged test file — hence the ~10,000 LoC
   of test work above.

Per SD-024 §Stage 2 (lines 281–286) and §Migration discipline (lines 222–256),
this bead should be split along the seam-swap boundaries the SD itself
prescribes. Concretely, the recommended split is:

runtimelint analyzer (cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer.go), comments
are not flagged — the analyzer fires on (1) forbidden field names on
*Runtime structs, (2) composite literals of legacy types, and (3) function
parameters typed as legacy options. None of those three patterns are
present in any of the three named files.

grade.go and service_run.go contain zero textual matches at all.

The work that satisfied these ACs landed in earlier beads:
  - 35eeebdd  refactor(agent): retire RunViaService/RunViaServiceWith/runFixtureHarnessViaRunner [ddx-b602ccce]
  - 68456595  refactor(agent): switch RunFunc + Run*With APIs to Runtime types [ddx-ce7a796e]
Both are ancestors of base-rev 2b34bfd0.

Verification of the build/test ACs
----------------------------------
- `cd cli && go build ./...` — clean, no output.
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -count=1` — the only failure
  is TestIntegration_CodexEcho, which fails on this same base-rev
  (verified with `git checkout 2b34bfd0~1 -- .` and re-running) with
  identical error:
      codex_core::session: failed to record rollout items:
      thread <id> not found
  This is an environment-level failure of the locally-installed `codex`
  binary's session-rollout subsystem, not a regression introduced by
  this bead's notional changes (which are zero). The same test was
  documented as a pre-existing environmental failure in the
  ddx-b602ccce no_changes_rationale committed at dfe1c545 a few hours
  earlier in this same session.

What the description asked for vs what AC verifies
--------------------------------------------------
The expanded description authorizes a full atomic migration of every
ddx-side caller off the three legacy options structs so that B22 can
trivially delete the type definitions in types.go. That broader
migration is NOT what the AC measures; the AC only measures absence of
the three names from three specific files plus build+test.

If the broader migration is the actual intent, it must be tracked by a
bead whose AC names the additional files (agent_runner.go,
agent_runner_service.go, claude_stream.go, virtual.go, script.go,
runner.go, run_internal.go, dispatch.go, models.go, session_index.go,
routing_metrics.go, runtime callers in execute_bead.go and
execute_bead_review.go, plus all *_test.go composite-literal callers).
The current AC's gate (the post-merge AC structural check from
ac_structural_check.go) will green-light this bead as-is.

Recommendation: close as already-satisfied. No code change is required
in this attempt.

What a follow-up would need
---------------------------
Only if the bead author intended the broader migration: re-open or
spawn a successor with AC items that actually name the call sites
(e.g. `field RunOptions removed from cli/internal/agent/runner.go`,
…/agent_runner.go, …/agent_runner_service.go, …/claude_stream.go,
…/virtual.go, …/script.go, …/run_internal.go, …/routing_metrics.go,
…/dispatch.go, …/models.go, …/execute_bead.go, …/execute_bead_review.go,
plus *_test.go composite-literal sites). With those AC items, the
runtimelint analyzer plus a clean build would prove the type
definitions in types.go:36/118/208 are unreachable, enabling B22's
final deletion.",
